title: Merge-gate operator audit trail — attestation package requested
preview:
From: Marcia Owens <marcia@shieldrisk.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: SIG Portal <sig-portal@sharedassess.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: operator audit trail for merge-gate (AtlasPay SIG)

Hi Ben,

As part of AtlasPay's SIG assessment we're validating your merge-gate controls and the operator-level audit trail for PR approvals that bypass automated gating. Specifically we need:

- A time-ordered export showing who triggered any manual merge during the last six months (username, timestamp, PR id, reason/approval reference).
- Evidence of role-based access for the merge operator (policy or role-binding).
- Keystore/credentials rotation logs demonstrating rotation events tied to the CI pipeline (if rotation impacts deploy keys).

If you can provide an attestation note and the supporting artifacts (CSV export, screenshots, short logs), that would satisfy the SIG question 3.2.1. We aim to close this control tomorrow EOD for the assessment window.

Thanks,
Marcia Owens
Senior Assessor — ShieldRisk
marcia@shieldrisk.com

P.S. If there's a recorded SOP that describes the emergency-merge authorization flow, attach that too.

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Marcia Owens <marcia@shieldrisk.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2027 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: operator audit trail for merge-gate (AtlasPay SIG)

Hi Marcia — thanks for the clear list. Attaching an initial export and a short attestation below; we'll follow up with the RBAC screenshot and keystore rotation snippet by tomorrow morning.

Attachment: merge_gate_audit.csv (columns: timestamp, operator, pr_id, repo, justification_tag)

Attestation:
Redwood Inference attests that for the period 2027-01-01 to 2027-06-30 the manual merge-gate (operator override) requests were limited to authorized engineers listed in the attached CSV. Manual merges require a documented justification and a second-line approval recorded in our deployment ticketing system (internal ref: DEPLOY-TICKET). Logs and role bindings are retained in our audit bucket for the customer assessment.

Please confirm receipt — Laura and Jordan, assigning to you to pull the role-binding screenshot and the keystore rotation log referenced below.

Best,
Ben Carter
Director, Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
+1 415 555 0187

From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2027 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: operator audit trail for merge-gate (AtlasPay SIG)

Thanks Ben — I pulled the CSV and spot-checked a few records. Jordan, can you: 

1) Export the IAM role-binding view for the CI service account and the merge-operator group (screenshot + JSON of the binding).
2) Grab the keystore rotation log from the KMS agent for the last 12 months and redact any key identifiers as needed (we only need timestamps and actor ids).
3) Confirm the SOP (we have a short-runbook) and attach the runbook PDF.

Marcia — we'll deliver the two artifacts and the runbook by 11:00 PT today. FYI the merge-operator role is limited to a small SRG and requires escalation approval recorded in Jira under DEPLOY-TICKET.

Laura Bennett
Manager, Customer Security Responses
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Marcia Owens <marcia@shieldrisk.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, SIG Portal <sig-portal@sharedassess.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2027 14:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: operator audit trail for merge-gate (AtlasPay SIG)

Marcia — attaching the promised artifacts: 

- merge_gate_audit.csv (exported from our deploy audit service)
- ci-role-binding-screenshot.png (console view of role bindings)
- keystore_rotation_log.txt (redacted rotation timestamps and actor ids)
- runbook_merge_override.pdf (SOP for emergency merge override)

Notes / quick summary:
- Manual merges are logged with PR id and a mandatory justification field; the CSV contains a justification_tag column tied to the Jira approval ticket.
- Role bindings show merge-operator membership scoped to the SRG (SRE + Release Eng leads).
- Keystore rotations occur quarterly; rotation events are recorded with a rotation_id and actor; we have redacted rotation ids for security but can provide a verifier hash if needed.

Let us know if you need an explicit signed attestation on Redwood letterhead — we can prepare one.

Best regards,
Ben

title: MapleStreet: High latency & 5xx surge — RCA, mitigations, exec readout
preview:
From: Elena Ruiz <elena@maplestreet.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Brian Ford <bford@maplestreet.com>, ops@maplestreet.com, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 09:15:12 -0700
Subject: Spike in latency + 5xxs impacting checkout (US-west)

Jordan —

We saw a large spike in end-to-end latency across our checkout service starting ~08:40 PDT and a surge of 500 responses from Redwood between 08:47–09:05. This impacted about 12% of traffic and caused retries on our side. We’ve attached a short timeline and sample logs.

Can your team confirm if this was an infra/regression issue on the Redwood side and what mitigations you have in place? This is blocking our morning releases — we need an initial readout and ETA for mitigation.

Elena Ruiz
Head of Platform Engineering
MapleStreet

Attachments: ms_latency_spike-graphs.pdf (pdf, 320KB)


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@maplestreet.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 09:23:55 -07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in latency + 5xxs impacting checkout (US-west)

Elena — thanks for the heads up and for the logs. We’re standing up an incident bridge now and I’ve looped in runtime/ops (Rishi, Grace).

Immediate asks: can you share a sample request ID (one or two) that hit 5xx so we can correlate? Also which model/route you were using (if you can paste the model id/route name that would help).

I’ll follow up with an initial triage within 30 minutes and a proposed mitigation. Apologies for the disruption.

—Jordan
VP, Enterprise Sales & Success
Redwood Inference


From: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena@maplestreet.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 09:46:31 -07:00
Subject: RE: Spike in latency + 5xxs impacting checkout (US-west)

On Thu, Jun 10, 2027 at 09:23 AM Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Elena — thanks for the heads up and for the logs. We’re standing up an incident bridge now and I’ve looped in runtime/ops (Rishi, Grace).

Quick triage summary (initial):
- Time window confirmed: 08:40–09:12 PDT.
- Symptom: sustained tail latency increase (p95 -> ~4.2s from baseline 220ms) and a correlated increase in model-inference 5xxs.
- Immediate probable cause: autoscaler latency on dedicated GPU pool + KV cache churn on the inference tier (higher miss rate after a short traffic pattern shift). When cache misses spiked, we saw backpressure onto the queue that caused token-timeouts in one serving shard, producing 5xx responses.
- Impact: ~11–13% of MapleStreet requests in that interval; increased retries drove a brief retry storm.

Attached: ms_latency_spike-graphs.pdf (graphs show shard 3 queue depth and cache miss rate).

Short-term mitigations we applied within the bridge:
1) Forced an immediate scale-up on the affected pool to add two warm workers (reduces queue depth).
2) Temporarily disabled aggressive prefix caching for MapleStreet route to avoid churn until we stabilize.
3) Enabled fallback to the smaller quantized model variant for requests that hit timeout thresholds.

Next steps: produce an RCA doc and runbook updates. I’m drafting a mitigation checklist and a recommended change to the autoscaler threshold. Will share in 2–3hrs.

—Grace
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference


From: Elena Ruiz <elena@maplestreet.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Brian Ford <bford@maplestreet.com>, ops@maplestreet.com
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 10:02:19 -07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in latency + 5xxs impacting checkout (US-west)

Thanks Grace — and thanks Jordan.

Appreciate the rapid mitigations. Two asks from our execs:
1) We need an executive-friendly readout and RCA sent to our CTO and CFO by EOD.
2) We’ll be formally requesting SLA credit for the affected window and need a statement of cause and confirmation of corrective actions before we submit.

Also, please include a timeline for code/config changes vs. operational runbook updates.

—Elena


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena@maplestreet.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 13:08:04 -07:00
Subject: Redwood executive summary & RCA draft — MapleStreet outage 2027-06-10

Elena — attaching the RCA draft and an exec summary. Per your asks, high level below: 

Executive summary (for CTO/CFO):- Incident window: 08:40–09:12 PDT (impacting ~12% of checkout calls).
- Root cause: a rapid traffic pattern shift caused elevated KV cache misses which, combined with our autoscaler thresholding on the dedicated pool, produced queue build-up and a serving shard timeout cascade that returned 5xx responses.
- What we did: forced extra warm workers, temporarily relaxed prefix-caching, enabled fallback to a quantized model variant for tail requests. Service restored to baseline by 09:18.
- Customer impact: transient increase in latency + retry-driven error spike; we identify no data leak or content corruption.

Planned corrective actions and owners (summary):
- Autoscaler tuning (reduce scale-up latency and add predictive headroom): Owner: Rishi Malhotra — ETA: config push 2027-06-17, testing 2027-06-16.
- Hot-shard mitigation (improve shard rebalancing and queue backpressure logic): Owner: Grace O'Connor — ETA: design review 2027-06-15, rollout 2027-06-24.
- Cache policy change (avoid aggressive prefix eviction on sudden pattern shifts): Owner: Aditya Rao — ETA: policy rollout 2027-06-14.
- SLA/credits statement and official post-incident letter: Owner: Laura Bennett (Legal & Finance liaison) — ETA: initial letter 2027-06-11 EOD.
- Runbook & alerting updates (clearer thresholds and auto-fallback): Owner: Rishi & Grace — ETA: 2027-06-14.

Attachments: maplestreet-rca-draft.docx, runbook-fallbacks.patch

Related links: https://drive.redwood.com/drive/MapleStreet/RCA-2027-06-10 (RCA draft), HubSpot: HS-45821 (deal record), Jira INF-7921 (autoscale & cache work).

Next steps: Laura will coordinate the credits letter; Rishi/Grace/Aditya will deliver code/config remediation timelines above. I’ll set a 30-minute executive readout tomorrow at 09:00 PDT to walk through the summary — please confirm availability.

—Jordan
VP, Enterprise Sales & Success
Redwood Inference


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena@maplestreet.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 13 Jun 2027 16:43:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Redwood executive summary & RCA draft — MapleStreet outage 2027-06-10

Jordan — confirming status against the action items and next steps. Apologies for the delayed confirmation; we prioritized stable mitigations then formalized the timeline.

Status update:
- Autoscaler tuning: branch created, load-sim tests pass on a mirrored pool. Config push scheduled 2027-06-16 will include reduced cooldown and an additive predictive headroom parameter.
- Hot-shard mitigation: design doc in review; we’ll add per-shard queue soft limits and async rebalancer; target staging rollout 2027-06-20.
- Cache policy change: policy patch will be pushed 2027-06-14 and includes a grace window for prefix cache retention to prevent eviction storms.
- Runbook updates: new alerting thresholds and auto-fallback to the quantized model have been committed to the runbook; PR linked in Jira INF-7921.
- SLA letter: Laura confirmed the draft and we’ll publish the official statement today (Laura to share).

Follow-ups I’ll own through closure: weekly check-ins with MapleStreet ops for the next 4 weeks and a post-change verification window after each rollout.

On Thu, Jun 10, 2027 at 13:08 PM Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Elena — attaching the RCA draft and an exec summary. Per your asks, high level below: ...

Thanks — Rishi
Senior Director, Runtime Engineering
Redwood Inference

title: Cross-cloud evaluation playbook + next steps (post-demo)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samantha Price <samantha.price@cloudforge.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Cross-cloud evaluation playbook + next steps (post-demo)

Team —

Quick recap from this morning's call with CloudForge (Samantha) and a tentative action plan. High-level takeaways:

- CloudForge is running initial comparisons against two hyperscaler inference endpoints (HyperscaleA, HyperEdge) and saw p95 deltas that worry their infra team for real-time scoring (p95 ~210–290ms on HyperEdge vs ~110–140ms on Redwood-hosted variant in our demo). They want a reproducible A/B run.
- Primary ask: a concise A/B protocol they can run (or we run jointly) to measure p95/p99 and sustained tokens/sec at 256/1024/2048 sequence lengths.
- Secondary ask: cost-per-million-tokens estimate across the configurations and a short write-up on fallbacks (model variants + quantization) that preserve latency.

Proposed next steps (please action):

1) Benchmark playbook draft (Marcus): produce a short YAML/config that codifies runtime variants, concurrency, payload shapes, and success criteria (p95 < 200ms for interactive use; p99 < 350ms). Include cold-start and steady-state runs.

2) Test matrix (Jordan): pick the 3 windows for a joint run with CloudForge next week — we should offer at least two time slots in their PDT window. Include a canary run that uses our quantized-8bit variant and a baseline float16 run.

3) Data & artifacts (Ben): share the call recording and initial CSV they sent, plus a HubSpot link & folder for artifacts. I'll coordinate logistics with Arjun at Edgecraft if we need alternate infra.

Bench metrics to capture (quick reference):
- p50 / p95 / p99 latency (server-side)
- tokens/sec (steady-state with concurrency sweep)
- end-to-end wall time (network + server)

title: Next steps: embedding pipeline decision log & function-call/streaming approach
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@novaly.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Discovery — embeddings + rerank + streaming funcalls (post-call recap)

Alex — thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap while it's fresh and a proposed set of decisions so we can lock an implementation path. TL;DR: you want a deterministic rerank/embedding pipeline for patient record retrieval, predictable latency for 95th pct, and a lightweight function-calling surface for structured output to downstream EHR processors.

Key pain points you mentioned:
- Search recall variability across long clinical notes (embeddings drift across updates).
- Token-level cost spikes during peak triage windows (evening PST).
- Need for a single, auditable structured output schema for downstream ingestion into your CDW.

Goals we aligned on:
1) Establish an embedding refresh cadence and indexing strategy that keeps recall stable while bounding costs.
2) Serve reranking at sub-200ms p95 for top-5 candidates for triage paths.
3) Deliver a function-call interface that returns validated JSON records (schema v1) for automated ingestion + an opt-in streaming path for long responses.

Proposed technical decisions (please flag anything you disagree with):
A) Embedding pipeline: incremental embedding updates every 6 hours for active patients, nightly full reindex for historical backlog. Use 1536-d quantized embeddings (recommended model) with similarity index sharding by facility.
B) Reranker: vector search + lightweight cross-encoder on top for top-20 -> top-5 narrowing; deploy on Dedicated capacity to meet p95 SLAs.
C) Function-calling: Redwood SDK function schema enforcement (we'll commit schema v1 and autoparse failures to a dead-letter queue). Include a small set of pre-defined transforms for Novaly (patient_summary, triage_score, evidence_pointers).
D) Streaming: optional streaming output for long narrative generation; default is non-streaming for triage paths to preserve determinism.

Next steps I propose:
- Ben to send a short mutual action plan (MAP) with dates and owners (EOD tomorrow).
- Novaly to confirm schema v1 fields and any PII redaction rules by EOD Thu.
- We will prepare a small SDK patch (diff) showing the function-call validation hooks and a sample streaming toggle.

Attachments: will share MAP and sdk change proposal in the follow-up.

Thanks — Jordan
Jordan Blake
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0199



From: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@novaly.io>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Novaly CTO Team <cto@novaly.io>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2027 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Discovery — embeddings + rerank + streaming funcalls (post-call recap)

Jordan — great summary and thanks. A couple of clarifications from our side: we can commit to delivering the schema v1 draft by Thursday, but we need to keep a conservative set of fields initially (patient_id, encounter_id, triage_level, summary_snippet, evidence_refs).

On the embedding cadence: 6-hour incremental looks reasonable for active patients. Two constraints: (1) our busiest facilities are in CST/EST so evening peaks are different, and (2) we need a way to mark records as sensitive so they are excluded from embeddings (PHI subset).

About latency: sub-200ms p95 for top-5 is ambitious but necessary. If Dedicated capacity is the approach, can you give a ballpark for the committed GPU profile and expected cost delta vs hosted per-token? We need that to build our business case.

Finally, quick ask — can we see a minimal example of the function-call JSON you mentioned (patient_summary) so our ingestion team can validate?

Thanks — Alex
Alex Moreno
CTO, Novaly Health
alex.moreno@novaly.io | +1 617-555-0123

On Mon, Jun 07, 2027 at 14:05 Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Alex — thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap while it's fresh...



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@novaly.io>
Date: Tue, 08 Jun 2027 08:10:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Discovery — embeddings + rerank + streaming funcalls (post-call recap)

Jordan / Alex — I attached the MAP draft and a short SDK patch that illustrates the function-call validation hook and streaming toggle. High-level answers to Alex's questions: 

1) Dedicated profile: estimate is 4x A10G-equivalent GPUs (sharded) for a predictable p95 under 200ms for top-5 across the triage workload we saw in your sample traces. Cost delta vs hosted is roughly a 1.7x uplift for steady-state (discounted further under an annual commit). I'll surface a detailed cost sheet once we have expected QPS.

2) Sensitive-record exclusion: we'll add tag-based filters in the pipeline. Novaly should provide a list of sensitivity tags and mapping rules (attached MAP has a placeholder).

3) Function-call sample (inline below) — this is the patient_summary response schema we propose in schema v1:
{
  \"patient_id\": \"string\",
  \"encounter_id\": \"string\",
  \"triage_level\": \"low|moderate|high\",
  \"summary_snippet\": \"string\",
  \"evidence_refs\": [\"ref_id1\", \"ref_id2\"]
}

Notes on streaming: SDK exposes a streaming=true flag — when set, Redwood will emit partial JSON events with a final commit event. For deterministic triage, keep streaming=false; for clinician-facing narrative, set streaming=true.

I'll update the MAP with owners and dates (Jordan, can you include expected QPS estimate from Novaly?).

-ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Attachments: MAP_Novaly_20270607.pdf, sdk_change_proposal.patch



From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@novaly.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 08 Jun 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Finalizing MAP & immediate asks (Novaly)

Alex — following Ben's notes, attaching the MAP and patch. Final, explicit asks so we can hit milestones next week:
1) Novaly: provide expected QPS (p95/mean) for triage queries and a list of sensitivity tags by EOD Friday (2027-06-11).
2) Novaly: confirm you can produce schema v1 draft (fields above) by Thu 2027-06-10 so we can implement validation in the SDK patch.
3) Redwood: Ben and I will circulate a cost appendix once we get QPS; we will also prepare a short performance test plan for a dedicated pilot.

If that works, Ben will schedule a 45-minute alignment next Tue to walk the Novaly engineers through the SDK change. I can do a short demo of streaming vs non-streaming behavior during that session.

Regards — Jordan

[Attachment: sdk_change_proposal.patch]

title: Invoice cadence + VAT evidence request — annual prepay
preview:
From: Elena Martínez <elena.m@polylinehealth.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-17T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Annual prepay — invoicing cadence and VAT evidence

Hi Connor,

We are preparing the paperwork for the annual prepay committed spend and need to lock down the invoicing cadence so our AP team can set up routing and PO numbers. Can you confirm:  

- Proposed invoice timing (initial invoice amount, frequency of drawdowns)
- What VAT evidence you require per country (we have EU VAT ID and local tax forms)
- Any PO number format you need or entity-specific remit instructions

Attached is our vendor onboarding form for context (Polyline_Onboarding.pdf). We also have a preference for monthly drawdowns if possible — please advise if that impacts unit pricing or SLOs.

Thanks,
Elena Martínez
Head of Finance Ops
Polyline Health
Attached: Polyline_Onboarding.pdf (PDF)


From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Elena Martínez <elena.m@polylinehealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-17T14:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Annual prepay — invoicing cadence and VAT evidence

Hi Elena — thanks for the form and the clear questions. High-level proposed flow below; happy to adjust based on your AP preferences.

1) Initial invoice: 50% of committed amount upon contract signature (SOW countersigned).
2) Remaining 50%: staged monthly drawdowns across the first 6 months (pro rata). We can convert to quarterly if you prefer fewer invoices.
3) Monthly drawdown notes: each drawdown invoice will reference the original contract id (DEAL-84932) and a simple PO format: POLY-<YYYY>-<PO#>. If your PO uses a different prefix we can map it.
4) VAT evidence: per-country requirements — we need either (a) gov-issued VAT registration certificate (VAT_Cert_[country].pdf) or (b) a Tax Exemption Letter plus local tax form. For EU entities the VAT ID is sufficient in most cases; local country GST proofs are required for AP reclaim.
5) Remit/KYC: bank remit instructions and beneficiary entity must match the invoiced legal entity. If you want netting across subsidiaries, we can set up a ledger mapping but that requires the legal entity list and W-8/W-9 equivalents.

I've attached an Invoice_Schedule_v1.pdf and a VAT_Evidence_Checklist.xlsx to show the exact docs we request per jurisdiction. I'll kick off a DocuSign SOW (link below) and can convert the schedule to quarterly if you confirm.

Next steps:
- Confirm PO prefix and legal entity for initial invoice
- Upload VAT certs for the invoiced entity (scan or PDFs are fine)
- Sign SOW (DocuSign link below)

Link: https://www.docusign.com/transaction/ABC123-Polyline-SOW

Best,
Connor O'Brien
Head of Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
Attached: Invoice_Schedule_v1.pdf, VAT_Evidence_Checklist.xlsx, SOW-Redwood-2027.pdf


From: Elena Martínez <elena.m@polylinehealth.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-18T09:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Annual prepay — invoicing cadence and VAT evidence

Connor — thanks, that reads well. A couple quick clarifications:  

- We’ll provide the PO prefix POLY-2027 and the initial PO# by next week.
- For the EU entity (Polyline EU B.V.) we will attach the VAT ID (VAT_Cert_NL.pdf). Do you accept a scanned PDF of the national VAT certificate?
- On netting: we'd like a single remit entity if possible; I will confirm legal entity and send the W-9 equivalent for our US parent.

If monthly drawdowns create too many invoices on your side we can accept quarterly — prefer monthly from an internal budgeting standpoint but can be flexible.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — Elena.


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-19T10:00:00-07:00
Subject: VAT evidence specifics for Polyline (internal)

Connor / Jordan — quick internal note on VAT evidence we should request from Polyline:  

- EU: VAT registration certificate (scanned PDF acceptable) + VAT ID string on invoice. Ask for VAT_Cert_NL.pdf for Polyline EU B.V.
- US: W-9 or W-8BEN-E as applicable; remit entity for US invoicing must match the TIN on W-9.
- AP wants bank remits and beneficiary name ahead of first invoice; request bank statement stub or signed remit letter if entity is new.

I'll prepare a short checklist email template we can send to Elena to make collection straightforward.

Kimberly Park
Head of Tax & Compliance
Redwood Inference
Attached: VAT_Request_Template.docx


From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Elena Martínez <elena.m@polylinehealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-21T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Annual prepay — invoicing cadence and VAT evidence

Hi Elena — following up and confirming the path forward:  

- We will invoice 50% on signature, remaining 50% billed over 6 monthly drawdowns unless you confirm quarterly.
- Please send the PO prefix and PO# when available (POLY-2027-XXXX).
- Upload VAT cert(s) for the invoiced entity; scanned PDFs are acceptable (VAT_Cert_NL.pdf for Polyline EU B.V.).
- Please attach W-9/W-8BEN-E for the US parent if you want US remit; we will match remit info to the entity on the invoice.

I've attached the Invoice_Schedule_v1.pdf and the VAT_Evidence_Checklist.xlsx again for convenience. For signature you can use the DocuSign link below — once the SOW is countersigned we will issue the initial invoice.

DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/transaction/ABC123-Polyline-SOW

If you'd like, I can have our AP specialist (Jordan) run through a short call to walk through the PO/GL mapping — happy to schedule.

Thanks,
Connor O'Brien
Head of Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
Attached: Invoice_Schedule_v1.pdf, VAT_Evidence_Checklist.xlsx

title: Furniture layout + power lane approval for Stand B12
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: sales@eventfurnish.com
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Furniture placement proposal for Stand B12 — needs approval

Hi team,

Attached is the first-pass furniture layout for Stand B12 (B12_CAD_v1.dwg). EventFurnish sent a layout and a quote but I flagged a few open questions I want us to decide on before we place the order:

- Demo counter location: currently on the left perimeter (A) but I think moving it to the front center (B) will improve sightlines for demos.
- Power lane orientation: vendor proposed a 90° run through the back; Moscone requires a main power lane with 1.5m clearance. Need to confirm routing so we can sign off on electrical work.
- Soft seating vs. bar stools: the quote includes both options. We should pick one to finalize quantities.
- Carpet color: charcoal vs. slate — marketing prefers charcoal, ops prefers slate (less visible dust).

Attachments: B12_CAD_v1.dwg (CAD), initial_layout_notes.txt (inline in drive).

Can each of you call out decisions or redlines by EOD Friday (May 21)? If we miss that window we risk the vendor’s 10-business-day lead time slipping.

Thanks,
Jordan
Jordan Blake
Field Marketing / Events
Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0132

Jordan — thanks for the file. Quick logistics notes from ops:

- Freight window for exhibitor move-in is Tue 6/29 0800–1600; if EventFurnish needs earlier rigging we need to pay for an early access pass.
- Moscone electrical requires final run diagrams 14 days prior to move-in (I attached the Moscone guideline PDF).

Naomi

Logistics Lead, Events
naomi.feldman@redwood.com

Hi Naomi / Jordan / Marissa,

Thanks for the quick turnaround. We updated the layout and quote per the weight and clearance notes — see EventFurnish_quote_v3.pdf and B12_CAD_v2.dwg (linked in the drive). Key confirmations:

- Demo counter assembled weight (including AV) = 260 lbs — within the platform limit but recommends center bracing if location changes.
- AV riser requires 2x 2\" floor bolts if placed off the main floor plates; otherwise friction mount is OK.
- Lead time for custom counters is 9 business days from PO receipt.
- We recommend the power lane run along the rear wall parallel to the service corridor (minimizes cross-traffic).

Pricing options included: soft seating package vs. bar stool package (prices shown separately). We can do carpet in charcoal (standard) or slate (+$30/stand).

If you approve the front-center demo counter and rear power lane we can lock pricing and ship to Moscone. PO to: accounting@eventfurnish.com

Regards,
Lara Mendes
Account Manager — EventFurnish Ltd.
sales@eventfurnish.com | +44 20 7946 0901

Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 09:22:00 -0700

title: Hosted API partial-request failures — status entry & customer action plan
preview:
From: Elena Cruz <elena@lilypad.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: support@lilypad.ai
Date: 2027-05-17T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected failures hitting API v1 - sample IDs attached

Hi Ben,

We started seeing a spike in partial failures to your hosted inference endpoint (api-v1.redwood.com/v1/generate) around 09:05 PDT. Rough sample: 20% of requests return truncated or empty payloads with a 200 + body {"error":"partial_result"} or occasionally 502 when we retry.

Examples (timestamps are PDT):
- request_id: 7b3f-11e0-9a23, 2027-05-17T09:06:13Z, prompt length 3200 tokens
- request_id: 7b3f-11e1-0b47, 2027-05-17T09:07:02Z, prompt length 6400 tokens

We've attached a short log snippet with full headers and the response bodies (lilypad_error_snippet.txt). Can you confirm whether this is a platform-side issue or if there's something we can tune on our side? Our SLAs are impacted and customers are seeing degraded UX.

We appreciate a status page link and an ETA.

Thanks,
Elena Cruz
Head of Platform — Lilypad AI
elena@lilypad.ai



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Cruz <elena@lilypad.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, support@lilypad.ai
Date: 2027-05-17T09:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected failures hitting API v1 - sample IDs attached

Hi Elena — thanks for the heads up and the sample IDs. We’re seeing a correlated signal in production and I’ve opened an incident on our status page: https://status.redwood.in/incidents/INC-20270517 (snapshot attached).

Immediate next steps (30m cadence updates):
- SRE (cc Marissa) is investigating root cause and running mitigations now.
- We’re routing affected traffic to a dedicated backfill pool to reduce impact on your requests.
- If you can, please temporarily limit max_tokens to 1k or pin to the compact model until we confirm resolution — we’ll clarify whether that’s necessary after triage.

Marissa — can you confirm preliminary findings and what we have running as mitigations? Jordan — looping you for customer comms/credits if needed.

Apologies for the disruption. We’ll keep updates coming every ~30 minutes on the incident page and via email.

—
Ben Carter
Director, Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
Phone: +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: statuspage-incident-snapshot.png



From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Elena Cruz <elena@lilypad.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, support@lilypad.ai
Date: 2027-05-17T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected failures hitting API v1 - sample IDs attached

Ben / Elena — preliminary technical update: 

Short summary: We observed transient kernel-level memory pressure on a subset of GPU hosts running the v1 serving stack when handling long sequences (>~4k tokens). That pressure caused partial request handling paths to return a short-circuited response for in-flight connections (manifesting as the partial_result bodies) and in rare cases a 502 when the rpc layer failed retries.

What we did (mitigations enacted 09:22–09:55 PDT):
- Enabled host spill (memory offload) for the affected pool to avoid kernel OOMs.
- Activated route-splitting to direct customers with long-sequence profiles to the dedicated cpu-backed fallback pool while we stabilize the GPU hosts.
- Increased the serving kernel’s robustness timeout and disabled one experimental kernel optimization flagged in the build for 2027-05-17.

Impact: ~18–25% of requests for sequences >4k tokens in the affected cluster experienced partial outputs between 09:05–09:40; after mitigations that dropped to ~2–3% and then to baseline by 10:00. Full clearance observed at 09:58.

Temporary customer recommendations:
- If feasible, set max_tokens <= 1000 or pin to model \"redwood-compact-v1\" until we confirm the permanent fix.
- If you need us to do the pinning/routing on your behalf we can apply it per-account (Jordan to coordinate).

We’ve attached a short orchestration log snippet showing the route split events (orchestration-log-snippet.txt). We will publish a fuller postmortem and timeline.

Marissa Cole
SRE Lead, Runtime
marissa@redwood.com

> On 2027-05-17 at 09:28, Ben Carter wrote:
> > Marissa — can you confirm preliminary findings and what we have running as mitigations?
> >
> > —
> > Ben



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Cruz <elena@lilypad.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, support@lilypad.ai
Date: 2027-05-18T14:55:00-07:00
Subject: Post-incident summary and next steps — INC-20270517

Elena — thank you again for the quick report and for the logs. Quick summary and next steps from Redwood: 

What happened: transient kernel memory pressure on a subset of GPU hosts handling long sequences caused partial-response behavior for a portion of v1 requests between 09:05–09:58 PDT on 2027-05-17.

What we fixed:
- Immediate: host spill and route-splitting mitigations reduced impact and restored baseline service by 09:58.
- Short-term: we reverted an experimental kernel optimization and increased timeouts for the next 72 hours.
- Planned: a targeted kernel patch and an automated detection rule for sequence-length-driven kernel pressure (deploy ETA: 2027-05-25).

Customer impact: Lilypad AI saw request-level partial-results for ~20% of >4k-token requests during the window. We’re proposing a one-time service credit for the incident window. Jordan — can you confirm the credit amount and coordinate with Elena?

Attached: postmortem-draft.docx (internal draft). Public postmortem will be published on the incident page once we finalize language and remediation dates.

Would you be open to a short 30-minute call tomorrow to review the remediation plan and any account-specific routing we can apply? Proposed times: 09:00 PDT or 11:00 PDT — let me know what works.

Again, apologies for the disruption. We’ll continue to monitor and will notify you of the kernel patch deployment date.

—
Ben Carter
Director, Sales Engineering | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
Phone: +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: postmortem-draft.docx

title: Clarification: audit export timestamps and sequence numbering
preview:
From: HarborBank SecOps <secops@harborbank.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Emily Ross <emily.ross@kcslegal.com>
Date: Sun, 16 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Audit export: timestamps + sequence numbers - clarification request

Hi Diego,

Thanks again for the call last week. We're putting together our procurement questionnaire and have a couple of specific follow-ups about exports of audit data from Redwood's hosted service:

1) Timestamp fidelity: do exported events include original event timestamps or only export ingestion timestamps? We need assurances that the original event_time is preserved and expressed in a stable timezone/format for cross-system correlation.

2) Sequence monotonicity: For forensic replay, we require a monotonic ordering token per event so we can guarantee replays reconstruct order within a partition. Does Redwood provide a sequence number or vector that is stable across export batches?

3) Manifest + checksums: We expect a manifest with per-file checksums and event counts for integrity checks during ingest on our side.

4) Retention windows & partial exports: If we request a bulk handoff for a specific date range (e.g., last 12 months), what are typical timelines and delivery options?

We've attached a short template (harbor_export_spec.xlsx) to show the fields we're comparing vendor responses on. Can you confirm the above items in writing for our SOC/Procurement review?

Appreciate it — Emily (on legal) is cc'd for any preservation language they want to add.

Best,
Marcus Lee
HarborBank - Security Operations
[Attachment: harbor_export_spec.xlsx (Excel)]

From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
To: HarborBank SecOps <secops@harborbank.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Ross <emily.ross@kcslegal.com>
Date: Sun, 16 May 2027 11:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Audit export: timestamps + sequence numbers - clarification request

Hi Marcus / Emily — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Jordan and Naomi so we can turn this into an export runbook you can use for SOC review. Quick answers up front, more detail below and a playbook PDF will follow:

- Timestamp: exports include the original event timestamp (event_time_utc) recorded by the service. We normalize to ISO-8601 (UTC) in every exported record; we also include an export_generated_at timestamp for traceability.
- Sequence: each event includes a "sequence_id" that is monotonic within a shard/partition. Exports preserve sequence ordering per partition and include partition_id so you can globally reconstruct ordering if you join by partition + sequence_id.
- Manifest/checksums: we produce a manifest.json alongside export files containing per-file SHA256 checksums and object-level event counts.
- Delivery/timeline: small ad-hoc extracts (<= 100GB) are typically available within 24-48 hours; larger handoffs follow our bulk delivery SLA and we provide presigned S3 URLs or SFTP + envelope manifest.

Jordan / Naomi — can you review the draft bullets below and add any technical specifics (hash format, event schema keys, parity guarantees) before I send to HarborBank?

Draft technical summary for HarborBank:
- Export format: newline-delimited JSON (ndjson).
- Key fields: event_id (UUID v4), event_time_utc (ISO-8601), sequence_id (64-bit int), partition_id (string), ingestion_time_utc, event_type, payload (redacted per retention policy).
- Monotonicity: sequence_id increases monotonically per partition; cross-partition ordering is not globally guaranteed.
- Checksum: SHA256 (hex), manifest tracks file_size_bytes + event_count.
- Retention: standard hosted: 90 days; Dedicated/Private options available for long-term retention and tailored export agreements.

I'll attach the internal draft playbook for your comments.

Best,
Diego Fernandez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: draft_playbook_v0.4.docx (Word)]

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 16 May 2027 13:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Audit export: timestamps + sequence numbers - clarification request

Diego — looks good. A couple of suggested clarifications to reduce follow-up: 

- Make explicit that event_time_utc is the producer timestamp (if available); where producer timestamp absent, we fall back to ingestion_time_utc and mark it in metadata.
- Recommend we call out the checksum algorithm exactly (SHA256, lowercase hex) and include a top-level manifest checksum for tamper-detection.
- Add a note about idempotency markers: we include an "origin_event_hash" (sha1 of canonicalized payload) to help dedupe on their side if they run partial replays.
- For large exports, propose a two-step delivery: 1) manifest + small sample validation file, 2) bulk transfer (S3 multipart). SLA: target bulk readiness within 72 hours for >1TB, with expedited options on Dedicated contracts.

If you want, I can paste a one-page schema snippet to include in the emailed reply.

—Jordan
Principal Product Manager, Redwood Console

From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
To: HarborBank SecOps <secops@harborbank.com>
Cc: Emily Ross <emily.ross@kcslegal.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 10:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Audit export: timestamps + sequence numbers - clarification request

Hi Marcus / Emily — thank you for the questions. Below is our agreed response and a short handoff checklist you can reference in procurement and SOC reviews. I've attached the customer-ready playbook (redwood_audit_export_playbook.pdf) with examples and sample manifest.

Executive summary (short):
- Exports contain the original event timestamp exposed as event_time_utc in ISO-8601 (UTC). Where a producer timestamp is unavailable we populate ingestion_time_utc and set a metadata flag (timestamp_source = "ingestion").
- Every event carries a sequence_id that is monotonically increasing within a partition_id. Exports are shipped in partition-preserving batches so you can replay events in-order by sorting on (partition_id, sequence_id). Global ordering across partitions is not provided.
- We ship a manifest file with per-file SHA256 (hex) checksums, file sizes, and event counts plus a top-level manifest_checksum to validate the whole bundle.

Delivery & retention specifics:
- Export formats: ndjson (newline-delimited JSON). Each line is one event.
- Field set (representative): event_id, event_time_utc, ingestion_time_utc, sequence_id, partition_id, origin_event_hash, event_type, payload.
- Typical hosted retention: 90 days. Dedicated/Private customers can contract longer retention and snapshot exports.
- Export timelines: ad-hoc extracts under 100GB: 24-48 hours; 100GB-1TB: 48-72 hours; >1TB: per-contract (standard target 72 hrs +). Expedited transfers available on Dedicated plans.
- Delivery channels: presigned S3 + manifest.json, SFTP (FTPS), or secure physical export on request (rare).

Integrity & replay controls:
- Checksums: SHA256, lowercase hex.
- Idempotency: origin_event_hash provided to detect duplicates during partial replays.
- Sampling: we can provide sample slices and accompanying verification steps prior to full handoff.

Handoff checklist (one-line items you can paste into your RFP sheet):
- Export format: ndjson (newline-delimited JSON)
- Timestamp field: event_time_utc (ISO-8601 UTC) + export_generated_at
- Ordering: partition_id + sequence_id (monotonic per partition)
- Manifest: manifest.json with per-file SHA256, file_size_bytes, event_count, manifest_checksum
- Delivery options: presigned S3 URLs / SFTP / expedited Dedicated transfer
- Typical timelines: 24-72 hours depending on size; contract SLA for >1TB

Attachments included: redwood_audit_export_playbook.pdf (customer playbook + sample manifest)

Next steps: If this addresses your needs we can produce a short appendix with sample ndjson rows and a one-click validation script for HarborBank to run during initial ingest. Happy to set a 30-minute walkthrough with your infra team this week.

Best regards,
Diego Fernandez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-6210
[Attachment: redwood_audit_export_playbook.pdf (PDF)]

text:
From: HarborBank SecOps <secops@harborbank.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Emily Ross <emily.ross@kcslegal.com>
Date: Sun, 16 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Audit export: timestamps + sequence numbers - clarification request

Hi Diego,

Thanks again for the call last week. We're putting together our procurement questionnaire and have a couple of specific follow-ups about exports of audit data from Redwood's hosted service:

1) Timestamp fidelity: do exported events include original event timestamps or only export ingestion timestamps? We need assurances that the original event_time is preserved and expressed in a stable timezone/format for cross-system correlation.

2) Sequence monotonicity: For forensic replay, we require a monotonic ordering token per event so we can guarantee replays reconstruct order within a partition. Does Redwood provide a sequence number or vector that is stable across export batches?

3) Manifest + checksums: We expect a manifest with per-file checksums and event counts for integrity checks during ingest on our side.

4) Retention windows & partial exports: If we request a bulk handoff for a specific date range (e.g., last 12 months), what are typical timelines and delivery options?

We've attached a short template (harbor_export_spec.xlsx) to show the fields we're comparing vendor responses on. Can you confirm the above items in writing for our SOC/Procurement review?

Appreciate it — Emily (on legal) is cc'd for any preservation language they want to add.

Best,
Marcus Lee
HarborBank - Security Operations
[Attachment: harbor_export_spec.xlsx (Excel)]

From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
To: HarborBank SecOps <secops@harborbank.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Emily Ross <emily.ross@kcslegal.com>
Date: Sun, 16 May 2027 11:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Audit export: timestamps + sequence numbers - clarification request

Hi Marcus / Emily — thanks for the clear list. Looping in Jordan and Naomi so we can turn this into an export runbook you can use for SOC review. Quick answers up front, more detail below and a playbook PDF will follow:

- Timestamp: exports include the original event timestamp (event_time_utc) recorded by the service. We normalize to ISO-8601 (UTC) in every exported record; we also include an export_generated_at timestamp for traceability.
- Sequence: each event includes a "sequence_id" that is monotonic within a shard/partition. Exports preserve sequence ordering per partition and include partition_id so you can globally reconstruct ordering if you join by partition + sequence_id.
- Manifest/checksums: we produce a manifest.json alongside export files containing per-file SHA256 checksums and object-level event counts.
- Delivery/timeline: small ad-hoc extracts (<= 100GB) are typically available within 24-48 hours; larger handoffs follow our bulk delivery SLA and we provide presigned S3 URLs or SFTP + envelope manifest.

Jordan / Naomi — can you review the draft bullets below and add any technical specifics (hash format, event schema keys, parity guarantees) before I send to HarborBank?

Draft technical summary for HarborBank:
- Export format: newline-delimited JSON (ndjson).
- Key fields: event_id (UUID v4), event_time_utc (ISO-8601), sequence_id (64-bit int), partition_id (string), ingestion_time_utc, event_type, payload (redacted per retention policy).
- Monotonicity: sequence_id increases monotonically per partition; cross-partition ordering is not globally guaranteed.
- Checksum: SHA256 (hex), manifest tracks file_size_bytes + event_count.
- Retention: standard hosted: 90 days; Dedicated/Private options available for long-term retention and tailored export agreements.

I'll attach the internal draft playbook for your comments.

Best,
Diego Fernandez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: draft_playbook_v0.4.docx (Word)]

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>

Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 16 May 2027 13:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Audit export: timestamps + sequence numbers - clarification request

Diego — looks good. A couple of suggested clarifications to reduce follow-up: 

- Make explicit that event_time_utc is the producer timestamp (if available); where producer timestamp absent, we fall back to ingestion_time_utc and mark it in metadata.
- Recommend we call out the checksum algorithm exactly (SHA256, lowercase hex) and include a top-level manifest checksum for tamper-detection.
- Add a note about idempotency markers: we include an "origin_event_hash" (sha1 of canonicalized payload) to help dedupe on their side if they run partial replays.
- For large exports, propose a two-step delivery: 1) manifest + small sample validation file, 2) bulk transfer (S3 multipart). SLA: target bulk readiness within 72 hours for >1TB, with expedited options on Dedicated contracts.

If you want, I can paste a one-page schema snippet to include in the emailed reply.

—Jordan
Principal Product Manager, Redwood Console

From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwoodinference.com>
To: HarborBank SecOps <secops@harborbank.com>
Cc: Emily Ross <emily.ross@kcslegal.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 10:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Audit export: timestamps + sequence numbers - clarification request

Hi Marcus / Emily — thank you for the questions. Below is our agreed response and a short handoff checklist you can reference in procurement and SOC reviews. I've attached the customer-ready playbook (redwood_audit_export_playbook.pdf) with examples and sample manifest.

Executive summary (short):
- Exports contain the original event timestamp exposed as event_time_utc in ISO-8601 (UTC). Where a producer timestamp is unavailable we populate ingestion_time_utc and set a metadata flag (timestamp_source = "ingestion").
- Every event carries a sequence_id that is monotonically increasing within a partition_id. Exports are shipped in partition-preserving batches so you can replay events in-order by sorting on (partition_id, sequence_id). Global ordering across partitions is not provided.
- We ship a manifest file with per-file SHA256 (hex) checksums, file sizes, and event counts plus a top-level manifest_checksum to validate the whole bundle.

Delivery & retention specifics:
- Export formats: ndjson (newline-delimited JSON). Each line is one event.
- Field set (representative): event_id, event_time_utc, ingestion_time_utc, sequence_id, partition_id, origin_event_hash, event_type, payload.
- Typical hosted retention: 90 days. Dedicated/Private customers can contract longer retention and snapshot exports.
- Export timelines: ad-hoc extracts under 100GB: 24-48 hours; 100GB-1TB: 48-72 hours; >1TB: per-contract (standard target 72 hrs +). Expedited transfers available on Dedicated plans.
- Delivery channels: presigned S3 + manifest.json, SFTP (FTPS), or secure physical export on request (rare).

Integrity & replay controls:
- Checksums: SHA256, lowercase hex.
- Idempotency: origin_event_hash provided to detect duplicates during partial replays.
- Sampling: we can provide sample slices and accompanying verification steps prior to full handoff.

Handoff checklist (one-line items you can paste into your RFP sheet):
- Export format: ndjson (newline-delimited JSON)
- Timestamp field: event_time_utc (ISO-8601 UTC) + export_generated_at
- Ordering: partition_id + sequence_id (monotonic per partition)
- Manifest: manifest.json with per-file SHA256, file_size_bytes, event_count, manifest_checksum
- Delivery options: presigned S3 URLs / SFTP / expedited Dedicated transfer
- Typical timelines: 24-72 hours depending on size; contract SLA for >1TB

Attachments included: redwood_audit_export_playbook.pdf (customer playbook + sample manifest)

Next steps: If this addresses your needs we can produce a short appendix with sample ndjson rows and a one-click validation script for HarborBank to run during initial ingest. Happy to set a 30-minute walkthrough with your infra team this week.

Best regards,
Diego Fernandez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-6210
[Attachment: redwood_audit_export_playbook.pdf (PDF)]

title: Renewal discussion: temporal billing smoothing & commit restructure
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira@polarishealth.com>; Raj Patel <raj.p@polarishealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Renewal discussion: temporal billing smoothing & commit restructure

Hi Samira / Raj —

Thanks for the note last week about seasonality in transcription volume. We ran the usage profile with the ops team and put together a renewal draft that tries to balance a stable committed rate with a short-term smoothing buffer for the anticipated summer spike.

Attachment: Polaris_Renewal_Proposal_v1.pdf (proposal: commit tiers, burst buffer, conditional crediting)

High level:
- 12-month term with a quarterly true-up.
- Base commit reduced 15% in Q1 to reflect lower baseline usage, with a temporary burst pool for Q2–Q3 sized to your forecast.
- If actual monthly usage exceeds burst pool by >20% for two consecutive months, automatic overage pricing applies (standard overage rate) — we included a one-time mitigated credit for the first month of breach.

Questions for you:
1) Can Procurement confirm whether a single PO for the 12-month invoice cadence is preferred?
2) Would a one-month SLA credit be acceptable if we miss the 99th percentile latency target during your go-live spike window?

Jordan will cover the ops sizing details on the call tomorrow. Happy to rework numbers after your feedback.

— Ben
Head of Mid-Market Sales
Redwood Inference


From: Samira Khan <samira@polarishealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj.p@polarishealth.com>; Procurement <procurement@polarishealth.com>
Date: 2027-05-01T12:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion: temporal billing smoothing & commit restructure

Ben —

Appreciate the quick turn. We reviewed the proposal with engineering and procurement. A few points:
- The burst pool looks directionally right but we need a guarantee that burst capacity is available in us-west for at least two weeks around our high-volume run (Jul 10–25).
- Procurement prefers quarterly invoices, not annual. Is that compatible with the commit structure?
- The SLA credit as written is fine, but we also need a shortfall credit if inference accuracy drops from baseline after the model swap (we can document the eval set).

I uploaded the internal forecast and a short note on the eval set (see: Polaris_Renewal_Redlines.docx).

—Samira
Director, AI Ops
Polaris Health


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-02T08:03:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Renewal discussion: temporal billing smoothing & commit restructure

Ben / Marissa —

Forwarding Samira's notes. Ops points: we can reserve burst nodes in us-west for Jul 10–25 but need a short lead window to scale the pool (minimum 14 days notice before activation). The burst pool in the proposal was modeled on the median of their forecast + 30% buffer; if procurement goes quarterly we can prorate quarterly invoices against the annual commitment.

Risks: if they opt out of annual precommit we lose the discount ladder beyond 6 months. If that's a blocker, recommend a hybrid: quarterly invoicing with a signed annual commitment letter (legal & procurement commit) — keeps our reorder economics intact but meets their AP cadence.

— Jordan
Solutions Engineer


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samira Khan <samira@polarishealth.com>; Raj Patel <raj.p@polarishealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-03T10:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion: temporal billing smoothing & commit restructure

Samira —

Thanks — a couple quick clarifications after ops review:
- We can lock burst capacity in us-west for Jul 10–25. To guarantee it we need written notice by Jun 25 and a non-refundable staging deposit equal to one week of burst capacity. We put that in the proposal so it's explicit.
- Quarterly invoicing: we can invoice quarterly and keep the full-year commit on the contract; invoicing cadence does not have to equal the commitment cadence. We'll call that out in the Contract Annex.
- For the accuracy shortfall protection: we'll add a three-month monitoring window after model change. If NLU accuracy (per their eval set) falls >2.5% absolute below the baseline during that window, we provide a pro-rata credit on the affected months (formula in the annex).

Next steps: I'll send an updated proposal with the deposit language and the accuracy shortfall methodology. If Procurement is comfortable with the quarterly invoice + annual commit letter, Legal can push a signature.

— Ben


From: Raj Patel <raj.p@polarishealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>; Samira Khan <samira@polarishealth.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@polarishealth.com>
Date: 2027-05-04T09:55:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion: temporal billing smoothing & commit restructure

Ben / Samira —

Procurement reviewed Jordan's hybrid suggestion and is amenable to:
1) A signed annual commitment letter (outlines committed units, term, and discount schedule).
2) Quarterly invoicing against that commitment (standard net-30).
3) The staging deposit is acceptable but Procurement asks for a cap: refundable (less actual usage) if burst is not activated.

We attached a short redline doc with two language requests (payment cap and dispute cadence) — see Polaris_Renewal_Redlines.docx. If you can accept, Procurement is ready to route to Legal.

— Raj
Director, Procurement
Polaris Health


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Raj Patel <raj.p@polarishealth.com>; Samira Khan <samira@polarishealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>; Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-05T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion: temporal billing smoothing & commit restructure

Raj —

Appreciate the redlines. We can agree to the refundable staging deposit cap (refund less actual utilized burst if not activated) and will add a 30-day dispute cadence to the Contract Annex. I'll circulate an updated proposal and a cover note for Legal/E-sign.

Action: Ben to upload updated proposal and DocuSign envelope by EOD 2027-05-06.

— Ben

Hi Samira, Jordan —

Thanks for looping me in. Attached below is a short first-pass set of talking points and interview questions. I kept it tight so the final film stays under 3 minutes.

Key narrative arc (3 bullets):
- The challenge: scaling patient experience while keeping inference cost predictable
- The solution: how Redwood routing/quantization/caching cut costs and stabilized latency in production
- The outcome: measurable wins (percent cost reduction, latency p99 improvement, and improved CSAT)

Suggested openers (one-sentence soundbites):
- "We needed predictable latency at scale so our patient experience felt instant — Redwood helped make that reliable without breaking the budget."
- "The drop in inference cost let us prioritize product features that matter most to patients."

Interview questions (short):
1) What was the core problem you wanted to solve?
2) Why did you choose Redwood over building internally/other providers?
3) Can you share specific metrics or outcomes?
4) Any memorable customer-facing wins after the rollout?

Logistics notes: we typically record two takes per question, capture 15–30s B-roll of the product and office, and collect a signed release from each on-camera participant.

If you want to tweak tone (more technical vs more customer-facing), tell me and I’ll adapt the script.

—Marissa

title: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@greenbank.com>, ops@cloudops.dev
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades

Hi Rajesh / CloudOps team,

Following our discovery last week, I'm proposing a lightweight, auditable approach for version pinning and scheduled maintenance for your private VPC deployment of Redwood. Goal is to minimize customer-side network change windows while giving us deterministic rollback and audit trails for compliance.

Attachments: vpc_pin_matrix_v2.xlsx (matrix with proposed pins & staging order)

Quick summary of asks:
- Please confirm blackout windows / preferred maintenance windows for April->June.
- Confirm whether GreenBank can accept a 2 hour window with a 30-minute canary & 90-minute progressive shift.
- Any firewall/ACL freeze windows or change control restrictions we need to know about (NOC/Change board times).

High-level proposal (attached matrix):
- Canary cluster: pin to 2.15.x (latest minor) for 24–48h validation.
- Production pin: initialize to 2.14.1 (current tested baseline) and schedule a controlled bump to 2.15.1 only after green canary signals.
- Rollback: automatic traffic cutback to pinned 2.14.1 and KV cache restore; database/data-plane schema no-ops.

I'll coordinate runbooks, health checks, and a final ASM (approval) doc once we lock a window. Jordan / Dev — looping you for canary plan and monitoring hooks.

Thanks,
Camila Reyes
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
mobile: +1-415-555-0129

---



From: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@greenbank.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, ops@cloudops.dev
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades

Camila — thanks for the summary. A few items from our side: 

- Preferred maintenance window: Tue/Wed 02:00–04:00 UTC (local banking hours unaffected). We can do a Saturday window if an emergency full-restart is needed, but prefer weekday overnight for monitoring staff onsite.
- Change freeze: our infra change freeze for transaction systems is 00:00–06:00 UTC Sundays; we cannot touch ACLs in that period. That said, ACLs can be scheduled during business days with 48h notice.
- Approvals: I will submit a Change Request to CAB; please provide the canary checklist and rollback steps to attach.

We'll review the attached matrix and give a firm OK by EOD Friday.

Rajesh
Infra Ops | GreenBank Financial

On Tue, Apr 20, 2027 at 09:12 AM Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Following our discovery last week, I'm proposing a lightweight, auditable approach...



From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@greenbank.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 10:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades

Camila / Jordan — quick internal note while Rajesh reviews:

I've sketched a canary plan to attach to the CAB packet. Key points:
- Canary scope = 5% production traffic for 2.15.x on isolated pool (no access to PII indices).
- Telemetry: latency p95, token error-rate, auth failures, and packet-drop from the VPC egress counters.
- Fallback automation: immediate route shift (traffic-proxy flag) + orchestration to rehydrate KV cache from snapshot.
- Manual gate at 30 minutes with explicit "proceed" from on-call SRE.

Attachment: canary-checklist.md (checklist with probe endpoints and play-by-play)

Also: we need Jordan to confirm we can perform the rota shift without touching customer-managed ACLs (we should rely on our egress NAT/ENI config). If ACL changes are required, CAB will need 72h notice.

-Dev



From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@greenbank.com>, ops@cloudops.dev
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades

Thanks for the quick turn, Rajesh. Notes after internal alignment: 

1) Pinning decisions:
- Production: we will pin production clusters to 2.14.1 for the immediate baseline.
- Canary: target 2.15.1 for canary pool. If canary passes for 24h (no regressions), a controlled roll to 2.15.1 for production will be scheduled but must be explicitly approved by GreenBank CAB.

2) Rollback mechanics (brief):
- If canary or early production signals breach thresholds (p95 latency +50ms or token errors >0.5%), we execute traffic proxy rollback to 2.14.1.
- KV restore: automated snapshot taken pre-cut. Snapshot retention TTL 48h for rollback window.
- Post-rollback verification plan: smoke write/read on <sanitized-test-queue> + end-to-end test run.

3) Network constraints:
- We will avoid customer ACL edits. Traffic shifts will be done at our egress layer (no ACL change needed). If ACLs are unavoidable, we will request 72h CAB approval.

4) Operational commitments:
- Monitoring hooks: Dev will expose the canary metrics dashboard and set three alert thresholds.
- DR rehearsals: Tessa to schedule a dry-run of rollback without traffic impact (dry-run uses staging tenants).

Next steps: Rajesh to confirm the CAB timeline and whether the Tuesday 02:00–04:00 UTC window is acceptable for the first canary attempt on 2027-04-30. After CAB ack, we'll send the final maintenance-runbook bundle (incl. canary-checklist.md, vpc_pin_matrix_v2.xlsx).

Camila

title: SI-cofunded trial credit routing & redemption matrix
preview:
From: Maya Singh <maya@nimbleworks.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Cofunded trial credits for CloudMarket listing - routing ask

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the intro call yesterday. NimbleWorks is ready to list Redwood on CloudMarket next month and we'd like to co-fund trials for our mutual customers. Before we finalize the marketplace listing, legal and billing asked for a short routing plan: how customers redeem SI-sponsored credits vs Redwood promotional credits, and whether credits can be top-up applied in the marketplace flow.

Attached is a draft "credit-allocation-matrix.xlsx" showing three scenarios (new customers, proof-of-concept, and expansion pilots) and proposed redemption windows (30/60/90 days). Can you confirm Redwood can: 1) accept partner-issued voucher codes that map to a marketplace trial product, and 2) apply partner cofunding first, with Redwood covering any remaining credits up to the trial cap?

If that flow works, we can get procurement to sign the co-funding agreement and push the DocuSign envelope. If not, please advise what changes you need (routing by HubSpot deal id, different expiration, etc.).

Thanks — looking to finalize by next Tuesday to meet marketplace deadlines.

Best,
Maya
Partnerships Manager, NimbleWorks

Attachment: credit-allocation-matrix.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Maya Singh <maya@nimbleworks.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cofunded trial credits for CloudMarket listing - routing ask

Maya —

Appreciate the matrix; clear and very helpful. Short answers below, longer notes after:

1) Partner-issued voucher codes: yes, we can support external partner voucher codes via the marketplace SKU flow, provided we receive the code list and mapping to HubSpot deal ids before listing.
2) Routing order: we can prioritize partner cofunding first, then Redwood credits to top up to the trial cap. We implement this via a two-step redemption call (partner-code validate -> apply partner credit; if remaining balance, apply redwood-promocode).

Longer notes / operational constraints:
- Voucher expiry must be a hard date; our marketplace instrument enforces expiration server-side.
- For auditing we need a CSV of issued codes with partner contact and intended HubSpot deal id.
- Top-ups: Redwood promo top-ups are allowed up to a per-account limit; we'll flag any top-ups above $X for manual approval.

Ben, Jordan — tagging you to confirm the approval channel for manual top-ups and whether we need a special SLA on activation times for marketplace redemptions.

I can loop legal if NimbleWorks needs the short consent clause in the cofunding agreement.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Partnerships - Redwood Inference

> On Thu, 22 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700, Maya Singh <maya@nimbleworks.com> wrote:
> > [attachment: credit-allocation-matrix.xlsx]

To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 13:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cofunded trial credits for CloudMarket listing - routing ask

Ava / Jordan —

Good with the flow in principle. A few operational points from billing:
- We need the partner-issued codes CSV (code, amount, expiry, external_contact, related_hubspot_deal_id) at least 48 hours before a batch goes live.
- Manual top-up approvals should be handled by CS for amounts > $2k; assign to Jordan's queue (CS-Partner-Topups).
- Please note: marketplace redemptions currently run nightly; if NimbleWorks needs same-day activation we'll need to route through an activation webhook (adds complexity).

Jordan — can you confirm whether CS can accept the 48-hour SLA and pick up top-ups > $2k? If not, we should set expectations with NimbleWorks.

Thanks,
Ben
Head of Revenue Ops, Redwood Inference

[signature snip]

Date: Fri, 23 Apr 2027 09:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cofunded trial credits for CloudMarket listing - routing ask

We can accept the 48-hour lead time and handle manual top-ups > $2k. Operational ask: please include the HubSpot deal id in the CSV so we auto-associate redemptions to the correct account.

Also: NimbleWorks' legal asked for an explicit line in the cofunding agreement that partner-issued credits are refundable only to NimbleWorks (not end customers). We've added a short clause to the attached PDF; please review and sign if good.

For administrative purposes I've uploaded a draft signed PDF placeholder in our shared Drive (link below), and I'll send a DocuSign envelope once you confirm.

Jordan / Ava —

Thanks — all sounds good and the refund clause is acceptable. I sent the DocuSign request this morning; envelope id 78912. Our billing team attached their preferred code list and HubSpot mappings in the DocuSign packet. Please confirm once signed and we'll schedule the marketplace listing for Wednesday.

Best,
Maya

- CS will monitor redemptions for first 72 hours and surface anomalies.

title: TrustCenter adoption — impact assessment and procurement timeline
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: TrustCenter adoption — impact assessment and procurement timeline

Hi Marissa / Stephanie,

We need a short impact assessment for adopting TrustCenter's Trust Portal across our hosted and private customers. This is coming up because the current Vanta contract for our site-wide subscription expires in July and we need a clear recommendation (keep Vanta, move to TrustCenter, or hybrid) with procurement steps. Please include cost delta, implementation effort, and any compliance/regulatory risks for EU customers.

Specific asks: 
- Marissa: can you run a quick cost model (annual TCO) comparing our current Vanta spend + SRE integration hours vs the TrustCenter quote (attached) and a hybrid where we keep Vanta for Dedicated accounts?
- Stephanie: can you map expected evidence collection gaps for our private deployment product (I've attached a starting evidence-gap matrix) and estimate internal engineering hours to remediate (per-control basis).

Timeline: I need a decision recommendation + redlined POs/contract points to legal by EOD Friday (2027-04-23) so procurement can vendor-lock by the end of May if we choose to switch before July.

Attachments: trustcenter-pricing-quote-2027-04.pdf, evidence-gap-matrix-2027-04.xlsx

Thanks —
Jordan
Jordan Blake
Head of Platform Partnerships, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: RE: TrustCenter adoption — impact assessment and procurement timeline
