title: Temporary concurrency exception and settlement path
preview:
From: Ethan Zhou <ezhou@fastretail.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Request: short-term concurrency increase for launch test

Camila —

Quick note: we’re running a simulated Black Friday load test next week and expect a concentrated 48–72h spike. Can Redwood provision a temporary concurrency lift (double our current concurrency from 100 -> 200) for the test window (UTC 2026-11-27 00:00 -> 2026-11-29 23:59)?

Two asks:
- Pricing: what would the overage / temporary headroom charge look like? We need a clear number to include in our PO.
- Discount/waiver: can you provide a one-off concession on overrun rates (or a cap) so we don’t see punitive invoice variance?

We’re prepared to commit to an additional month at the elevated commit if that helps with pricing — happy to discuss.

Procurement may need a drafted quote / redline for approval. I’ve looped them on this thread.

Thanks,
Ethan
Ethan Zhou
Director, Platform Engineering — FastRetail
E: ezhou@fastretail.com
T: +1 415-555-0142

Attachment: none


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Zhou <ezhou@fastretail.com>
Cc: FastRetail Procurement <procurement@fastretail.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 14:37:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: short-term concurrency increase for launch test

Ethan —

Thanks — we can certainly support a temporary concurrency exception. I’ll coordinate internally and get you a concise proposal (price + cap + invoicing path) by tomorrow EOD PST.

High-level options we typically offer:
- Short-term headroom: fixed flat fee for the window + standard overrun rate (pro-rated).
- True-up approach: grant provisional headroom, invoice for the uplift at an agreed flat blended rate, reconcile after the event.
- Commit swap: if you commit to an extra month of elevated capacity we can apply a partial discount to the uplift or cap the overrun.

I’ll include a pro forma quote and a redline-ready contract snippet for Procurement.

Camila Reyes
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0202



From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 15:05:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Request: short-term concurrency increase for launch test — pricing & approvals needed

Team —

Forwarding FastRetail’s ask (below). They want a 48–72h window doubling concurrency. They asked for pricing that Procurement can include in a PO and some protection on runaway overrun charges. FastRetail is high priority — this is a potential commit conversion (deal FR-2381).

Proposed approach (please comment):
1) Offer a provisional headroom package for 72 hours: flat uplift fee + reduced overrun rate for the first 48 hours.
   - Example framing: $6,000 flat uplift for 72h double concurrency (covers baseline increment), plus $0.10/token overrun for first 48h, then standard $0.16/token thereafter (example numbers — Ben, pls advise).
2) Cap: suggest a hard cap on total charge during the window (e.g., $15k) to give procurement predictability.
3) Legal: we’ll provide a one-page addendum and a redline to the current SOW that explicitly lists the window and invoicing. Markus, can you prepare a short redline and confirm any compliance impacts?
4) Finance: how should we invoice? Jordan, can we do a proforma invoice to attach to their PO and then true-up on the monthly invoice?

Action items:
- Ben: produce 2 price options (conservative & aggressive) and a proforma quote by tomorrow morning PST.
- Markus: quick redline and note on any legal/contractual risk.
- Jordan: confirm invoicing flow and whether we can apply a temporary cap in billing system.

I’ll consolidate and send a short customer-facing email once we have numbers.

Camila

[quoted original message]


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: short-term concurrency increase for launch test — pricing & approvals needed

Camila / team —

Draft numbers and recommendation below (aimed at balancing predictability for FastRetail and protecting Redwood from runaway exposure):

Option A — Predictable flat uplift (recommended)- 72-hour temporary double concurrency: flat fee $5,500 (covers baseline extra capacity).
- Overrun rate: $0.095 per token for first 48 hours, then $0.16 per token thereafter.
- Hard cap during window: $12,000 total.
- Benefit: Procurement gets a clean number for the PO; we avoid large month-end surprises.

Option B — Lower upfront, higher variable (cost-containment for Redwood)
- 72-hour provisional headroom: $2,500 flat fee.
- Overrun: $0.06 per token for the first X tokens (up to a threshold), then $0.20/token beyond.
- No cap (or very high cap). Not preferred from procurement perspective.

My pick: Option A paired with a single-line addendum. Attachments: proforma quote (FR-2381_proforma-quote.pdf) and a redline (FR-2381_contract-redline.docx) — Markus, please review redline. Jordan, confirm we can issue a proforma invoice and then true-up on Dec invoice.

Ben Carter
Sr. Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0303

Attachment: FR-2381_proforma-quote.pdf


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Zhou <ezhou@fastretail.com>, FastRetail Procurement <procurement@fastretail.com>

- Window: 2026-11-27 00:00 UTC -> 2026-11-29 23:59 UTC (72 hours)
- Provision: concurrency doubled from 100 -> 200 for the window
- Pricing (Option A, accepted): $5,500 flat uplift for the 72h window + $0.095 per token overrun for the first 48 hours, then $0.16/token thereafter
- Cap: hard cap of $12,000 total for the window (once cap hit, we will throttle back to baseline and notify you)
- Invoicing: we will attach a proforma invoice to your PO immediately and then true-up differences on the next monthly invoice (Dec 2026). Jordan Blake (billing) will coordinate the invoice schedule.

2) We’ll provision the temporary uplift at the start of the window; Ben will confirm once the resources are in place.
3) After the window we’ll provide a short usage report and reconcile charges.

Please reply with formal PO or let me know any final questions — happy to hop on a 15m call if helpful.

Thanks —

From: Ethan Zhou <ezhou@fastretail.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Request: short-term concurrency increase for launch test

Camila —

Quick note: we’re running a simulated Black Friday load test next week and expect a concentrated 48–72h spike. Can Redwood provision a temporary concurrency lift (double our current concurrency from 100 -> 200) for the test window (UTC 2026-11-27 00:00 -> 2026-11-29 23:59)?

Two asks:
- Pricing: what would the overage / temporary headroom charge look like? We need a clear number to include in our PO.
- Discount/waiver: can you provide a one-off concession on overrun rates (or a cap) so we don’t see punitive invoice variance?

We’re prepared to commit to an additional month at the elevated commit if that helps with pricing — happy to discuss.

Procurement may need a drafted quote / redline for approval. I’ve looped them on this thread.

Thanks,
Ethan

Director, Platform Engineering — FastRetail

From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Zhou <ezhou@fastretail.com>
Cc: FastRetail Procurement <procurement@fastretail.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 14:37:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: short-term concurrency increase for launch test

Ethan —

Thanks — we can certainly support a temporary concurrency exception. I’ll coordinate internally and get you a concise proposal (price + cap + invoicing path) by tomorrow EOD PST.

High-level options we typically offer:
- Short-term headroom: fixed flat fee for the window + standard overrun rate (pro-rated).
- True-up approach: grant provisional headroom, invoice for the uplift at an agreed flat blended rate, reconcile after the event.
- Commit swap: if you commit to an extra month of elevated capacity we can apply a partial discount to the uplift or cap the overrun.

I’ll include a pro forma quote and a redline-ready contract snippet for Procurement.

Camila Reyes
Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0202

Proposed approach (please comment):
1) Offer a provisional headroom package for 72 hours: flat uplift fee + reduced overrun rate for the first 48 hours.
   - Example framing: $6,000 flat uplift for 72h double concurrency (covers baseline increment), plus $0.10/token overrun for first 48h, then standard $0.16/token thereafter (example numbers — Ben, pls advise).
2) Cap: suggest a hard cap on total charge during the window (e.g., $15k) to give procurement predictability.
3) Legal: we’ll provide a one-page addendum and a redline to the current SOW that explicitly lists the window and invoicing. Markus, can you prepare a short redline and confirm any compliance impacts?
4) Finance: how should we invoice? Jordan, can we do a proforma invoice to attach to their PO and then true-up on the monthly invoice?

Action items:
- Ben: produce 2 price options (conservative & aggressive) and a proforma quote by tomorrow morning PST.
- Markus: quick redline and note on any legal/contractual risk.
- Jordan: confirm invoicing flow and whether we can apply a temporary cap in billing system.

I’ll consolidate and send a short customer-facing email once we have numbers.

Camila

- Overrun: $0.06 per token for the first X tokens (up to a threshold), then $0.20/token beyond.

title: Priority slot request: Nimbus billing-residency feature
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-18T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Nimbus: ask for priority slot for billing-residency feature

Team — quick context: Nimbus (enterprise healthcare customer) finished a procurement review today and Procurement signaled they want our billing residency option (tenant-level billing data residency in us-east/Gov-like isolation) baked into the contract schedule. They want it live before 2027-02-01 as a contractual milestone.

Why this matters: Nimbus is at $2.1M ARR annualized for year 1 and has indicated a multi-year commitment if we can meet the residency milestone. They also flagged SOC2 logs and audit/export cadence as must-haves but those are separate asks.

What I need from Product/Eng: internal prioritization for a small, scoped residency feature (billing data segregation + export path + audit flag) to be scheduled into our January sprint cycle. They are willing to sign a partial prepay (6 months) if we can agree to the milestone.

Attachments: nimbus-MAP-v2.pdf (deal summary, obligations), sla-redlines-20261115.docx (procurement redlines)

Can we get a triage decision + rough ETA by EOD Friday 11/20? If yes, I will coordinate a short alignment call with Nimbus (Alex Grant, Laura Chen).

—Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
Mobile: +1 415 555 0196



From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-18T11:09:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Nimbus: ask for priority slot for billing-residency feature

Thanks Ben — appreciate the context and the attachments. A few quick clarifying questions before we commit a slot: 

- Scope: when you say “billing data segregation” do you mean tenant-level tokenization/prefixing only for invoices and billing logs, or full tenant KV isolation (residency + networking/VPC) that would require infra changes?
- Cutover expectations: is Nimbus expecting a disruptive migration window or an opt-in toggle per tenant?
- Compliance: they flagged SOC2 and export cadence; do they require new audit events or just retention policy change?

Notes: Our January sprint is currently committed to two platform GA items (streaming route rewrite + cache refactor). We may have a single emergent slot for a focused 3–4 week initiative if the scope is narrow. If the ask is larger (network/VPC-level residency), timeline moves to 6–8+ weeks.

Please fill the priority-request-template.xlsx with a proposed minimal scope (MVP checklist) and target acceptance criteria; that’ll help Eng do a faster T-shirt estimate.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
VP, Product — Platform
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>, Alex Grant <alex.grant@nimbushealth.com>, Laura Chen <laura.chen@nimbushealth.com>
Date: 2026-11-19T08:21:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Nimbus: ask for priority slot for billing-residency feature

Marissa — answered inline and attached a filled copy of the template.

- Scope (MVP): billing export + invoice storage residency per tenant (S3 bucket per region + export tunnel), billing logs with residency flag. No VPC or private networking in MVP.
- Cutover: Nimbus prefers an opt-in toggle per tenant with a planned migration window; they want zero downtime for API surfaces.
- Compliance: they asked for additional audit events for export requests + 90-day retention override for billing artifacts (regulatory).

Attached: priority-request-template.xlsx (filled with MVP checklist)

Customer notes (for internal planning):
- Nimbus negotiation posture: very constructive; procurement wants the residency milestone codified but will accept an MVP scope.
- Business counter: they will prepay 6 months and sign a Letter of Intent contingent on milestone. CFO (Laura Chen) will need exact acceptance criteria in SOW.

Ben



From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-20T10:34:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Nimbus: ask for priority slot for billing-residency feature

Thanks all — reviewed Ben’s filled template. This looks like a narrowly scoped MVP (billing artifacts + export path + audit events) which is feasible without VPC-level work. Requesting the following next steps: 

1) Jordan/Karthik: can you provide a two-point estimate ("small/medium") and a best-effort ETA if we push this into the January emergent slot? We need a dev cycle estimate and a QA/security signoff window.
2) Marissa: if Product signs off on MVP acceptance criteria, I’ll escalate to the delivery board to reserve the slot and build the SOW language for SPM/legal.
3) Ben: please push for a formal written LOI from Nimbus (email + signed PDF) that commits to the prepay and milestone dates; procurement must see acceptance criteria in writing.

I’ve attached a suggested SOW snippet and the priority-request-template.xlsx with a couple of acceptance tweaks.

Attachment: priority-request-template.xlsx (updated), suggested-sow-snippet.txt

Chloe
Chloe Martin
Strategic Deals — Redwood Inference
chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com



From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-11-24T14:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Nimbus: ask for priority slot for billing-residency feature

Quick engineering readout — thanks for the clear template. My preliminary take: 

- T-shirt estimate: Small-to-Medium. Rough breakdown: 2 sprints of focused dev (one sprint for storage/export plumbing + flags, one sprint for QA/security and acceptance automation).
- Risks: audit-event instrumentation is straightforward, but export path requires careful IAM mapping and e2e verification. If Nimbus needs per-tenant S3 buckets with cross-account policies, that adds 1 sprint.
- Blockers: need final acceptance criteria from Product (Marissa) and confirmation whether the 90-day retention override needs to be enforced at platform level (policy engine) vs. application.

If Product signs the acceptance criteria by 11/30, and Delivery Board approves slot on 12/03, we can target a feature completion date of 2027-01-28 with a production toggle/limited rollout. If they require cross-account VPC/Networking or dedicated HSM-backed KMS, timeline extends to 6+ weeks.

Next: I’ll prepare a short engineering scope doc (1 pager) and a checklist for security signoff; Marissa/Chloe, I’ll drop that into the Drive link and ping the delivery board.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake
Senior Engineering Manager, Runtime
jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com

From: Laura Chen <laura.chen@gridara.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 15:12:00 -0500
Subject: Intro: Redwood demo + exec alignment

Ben / Jordan —

Thanks again for the inbound. Gridara is excited to see Redwood in action. Leadership here has asked that the demo include an executive-level intro so our VP of Engineering (Anita Rao) and CIO (Michael O'Neill) can attend. CTO is tentatively available but has a travel conflict on the 30th.

Proposed windows (all ET):
- Tue Nov 30, 10:00–10:45
- Tue Nov 30, 14:00–14:45
- Wed Dec 1, 09:00–09:45

I've attached our security questionnaire and a short note on their exec priorities. Can you propose who from Redwood will be the sponsor and who can cover if the CTO needs to drop? Also we prefer a 5–10 minute pre-brief with the execs 30 minutes before the demo.

Attachments: security-questionnaire.pdf

Thanks,
Laura Chen
Director, Strategic Accounts — Gridara Systems
laura.chen@gridara.com
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0199


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>

title: RetailFlux renewal: staggered bakeoff & capacity sprint
preview:
From: Emma Liu <emma@retailflux.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.p@retailflux.com>
Date: Thu, 05 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Renewal + benchmark ask ahead of Dec renewal

Diego —

Quick heads up: our renewal with Redwood lands 12/15 and we've just had an inbound from a potential replacement vendor (they did a demo yesterday). Procurement wants a formal throughput/latency test now rather than later. They said seeing a side-by-side will be part of the decision.

Can Redwood run a focused bakeoff that mirrors our production traffic (mix of short chat queries and long-form generation, ~60M tokens/day) and show 95/99p latency and cost delta? Ideally we can schedule a 2-day window in early November so we have results for our exec review.

Also: we need written confirmation of current committed nodes/throughput guarantees and whether a 6–12 month term with a capacity uplift discount is available. If you can pull together a short plan and dates that would help Procurement.

Thanks —
Emma Liu
Head of ML Ops, RetailFlux
[Attachment: Q3_throughput_snapshot.pdf]

From: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 05 Nov 2026 10:02:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Renewal + benchmark ask ahead of Dec renewal

Team —

FYI RetailFlux just asked for a focused side-by-side benchmark. Key asks: mirror production mix (short chat + long gen), target ~60M tokens/day, show 95/99p latency and cost delta, and provide term/discount options. They want a 2-day window in early Nov for results (exec review).

Initial thoughts: run a staggered bakeoff across two variants —
- Variant A: current prod model pinned + our prefix/KV caching enabled (simulate live traffic)
- Variant B: quantized model (gptx-7b-q4) + optimized batching (higher throughput)

We should include Dedicated capacity numbers and a contingency fallback (region failover or hybrid dedicated + hosted). Marissa — can you own the benchmark plan document and timeline? Karthik/Jordan — need ops/serving ETA and what data we need from RetailFlux to run a faithful test.

I'll reply to Emma with proposed slots and a short SOW.

— Diego
Diego Fernandez
Account Exec, Redwood Inference

From: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
To: Emma Liu <emma@retailflux.com>, Samir Patel <samir.p@retailflux.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 06 Nov 2026 08:17:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + benchmark ask ahead of Dec renewal

Emma —

Thanks for the note and the Q3 snapshot. We can run a 2-day staged bakeoff and will provide a short report showing latency percentiles, tokens/sec, and estimated per-token cost for each variant. Proposed plan (high-level):

1) Data & traffic profile: you send a 48-hour sample of anonymized request traces (headers only + token lengths) and target SLOs (95p / 99p desired numbers).
2) Test window options: Nov 16–17 OR Nov 23–24 (we have limited Dedicated windows early Nov due to other commitments). Please confirm preferred slot.
3) Variants we’ll run: Primary (current model + caching) and High-throughput (quantized + batch tuning). We will also run a short burst test on Dedicated to show scaling to committed nodes.
4) Deliverables: test plan (attached), results CSV, short exec one-pager, and a term proposal showing a 12-month capacity uplift discount option (example: 20% off incremental committed spend for 12 months if they commit to +30% capacity).

We can also sign a non-prod NDA if you prefer. I attached the benchmark plan and our perf template.

If Procurement needs a standard PO/contract timeline, loop in your procurement address and we’ll coordinate legal.

Options on pricing/term: we can offer a short-term bridge (3-month capacity uplift with a committable conversion) or a 12-month uplift with deeper discount and guaranteed priority capacity. We'll specify fallbacks and rollback windows in SOW.

Let me know which test window works.

Best,
Diego
Account Exec, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: retailflux_benchmark_plan.pdf][Attachment: throughput_summary_q3.xlsx]

From: Emma Liu <emma@retailflux.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.p@retailflux.com>, Procurement <procure@retailflux.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Nov 2026 11:43:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + benchmark ask ahead of Dec renewal

Diego —

Thanks, Nov 16–17 works for us. We will prepare an anonymized 48-hour trace and share by EOD Nov 12. Two quick asks from Procurement:

- Can Redwood confirm committed node count and the 99p latency target you believe you can demonstrate for our current workload? We need a written statement for our internal risk committee.
- If we sign a 12-month uplift, what's the earliest you can guarantee Additional Dedicated nodes for spike windows? We need the ability to burst 2x for Black Friday and then scale back.

Procurement (Procure@retailflux.com) — please confirm our internal dates for sign-off; we plan an exec review on Nov 25. If PO is going to be needed to hold slots, let us know and we’ll push with finance.

Emma
Head of ML Ops, RetailFlux

>
> Thanks —
> Emma


From: Diego Fernandez <diego@redwood.com>
To: Emma Liu <emma@retailflux.com>, Procurement <procure@retailflux.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 15:36:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: RetailFlux renewal: staggered bakeoff & capacity sprint — confirmation and next steps

Emma / Procurement —

Confirming Nov 16–17 test completed; summary below and attached full CSV and one-pager. Quick highlights for your exec review and Procurement:

- 95p / 99p latency for Primary variant (current model + caching): 95p = 82ms, 99p = 148ms (workload had several long-gen tails).
- High-throughput variant (gptx-7b-q4 quantized + batch tuning): 95p = 110ms, 99p = 160ms but delivered ~2.1x tokens/sec and reduced estimated per-token cost by 28% in our simulated profile.
- Dedicated burst: we validated scaling to +2x committed nodes; cold-starts for extra nodes were within the 90s window and can be automated with a scheduled burst policy.

Recommendation (Sales/Contract): a 12-month uplift with a +30% committed capacity buys a 20% incremental discount and priority burst capacity for Black Friday. If RetailFlux prefers a shorter runway, we can do a 3-month bridge with optional conversion.

Action items:
- Diego: share final one-pager and PO template.
- Procurement: confirm if a PO is required to reserve burst windows for Black Friday.
- Karthik/Jordan: include the failover runbook and scheduled burst automation snippet in the SOW.

Attached: retailflux_benchmark_plan.pdf, throughput_summary_q3.xlsx, purchase_order_RF-457.pdf

Thanks —
Diego Fernandez
Account Exec, Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0192
[Attachment: retailflux_benchmark_results_onepager.pdf][Attachment: purchase_order_RF-457.pdf]

title: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice Nguyen <alice.nguyen@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Alice — thanks again for taking the time yesterday. As discussed, ACME is preparing to onboard its first private deployment and we want to align on a recommended connectivity pattern that balances latency, operational ownership, and auditability.

I attached a short comparison (redwood-connection-comparison-v2.pdf) but high level the three paths we typically see are: 
- VPC peering (cross-account peering) — low-latency, simple routing but requires both sides to accept peering and route coordination.
- AWS PrivateLink (interface endpoints) — strong isolation and per-service endpoints, easier cross-account access controls, but introduces SNAT/egress considerations depending on your architecture.
- Site-to-site VPN (IPSec) — fast to stand up, supports on-prem and cloud, but higher latency/maintenance and less scalable at high throughput.

Could you confirm any hard constraints from ACME's side (account separation, must-route-through-hub, TLS termination preferences, requirement for flow/audit logs retention)? Once we have that I'll loop in Kimberly and Jordan for the deployment checklist and a pilot plan.

Next step suggested: 60-minute technical workshop this week to pick a primary and fallback path, with roles documented for NetOps and Redwood. Is Thursday 11/19 10:00 PT available?

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
attachments: redwood-connection-comparison-v2.pdf (15 pages), acme-network-requirements-20261116.xlsx (stub)


From: Alice Nguyen <alice.nguyen@acme-corp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: netops@acme-corp.com
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 13:44:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Ben — thanks for the summary and the docs. Short answers / constraints from NetOps: 
- We cannot accept VPC peering from third-party provider accounts into our production account due to lateral movement policy (we maintain strict account tenancy).
- PrivateLink is acceptable, but we need clarification on who manages the interface endpoints (ACME or Redwood) and how endpoint ENIs are tagged for billing and audit.
- We have an on-prem data center that will need access to inference endpoints for a pilot; we prefer VPN for on-prem -> cloud unless PrivateLink + Transit Gateway is recommended.
- TLS: all traffic must be TLS-terminated at the application layer (no early TLS termination at network edge).
- Logging/retention: flow logs and connection metadata must be retained for 365 days.

Thursday 11/19 10:00 PT works for the workshop. Please include your network architect and an on-call NetOps engineer; I'll invite ours.

—Alice
Alice Nguyen
Head of Cloud Operations, ACME Corporation


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice Nguyen <alice.nguyen@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 08:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Alice — great notes, thanks. Looping in Kimberly (security & compliance) and Jordan (network architecture) to address the ENI/management questions and the TLS/logging items.

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Alice Nguyen <alice.nguyen@acme-corp.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 07:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Alice / Ben / team — quick technical note ahead of the workshop to set expectations. TL;DR recommendation: primary = PrivateLink (interface endpoint) with Redwood managing the service endpoint; fallback = S2S VPN for on-prem access during the pilot. Rationale below.

Why PrivateLink (recommended):
- Cross-account isolation: works without peering and aligns with ACME policy.
- Security & audit: each endpoint ENI can be tagged and we can emit ENI-level flow logs and publish to ACME's centralized logging (we will coordinate IAM role assumptions and S3 export policy).
- Latency: single-digit to low-double-digit ms for most US regions vs peering which might be slightly lower but not allowed by policy.
- Operational model: Redwood can offer a managed endpoint model where we create the service in our control plane and ACME creates the interface endpoint in their VPC; we propose an automated Terraform module so both sides get idempotent, auditable config.

PrivateLink considerations / mitigations:
- Egress/NAT: If ACME needs outbound access to the public internet from the same subnet, we'll recommend explicit NAT routes or an egress proxy managed by ACME. We'll document SNAT interactions in the runbook.
- TLS termination: per requirement, TLS will terminate at the application (container) layer within Redwood; PrivateLink only carries encrypted traffic.
- Logging retention: Redwood will ship VPC Flow Logs and connection metadata to an ACME S3 bucket with lifecycle/retention policy set to 365 days. We'll include log delivery SLA in the SOW.
- Throughput limits: interface endpoints scale but we will provision tests; for high-volume workloads we'll add a parallel autoscale lane and provide a fallback via S2S VPN if line-rate requirements demand it.

S2S VPN as fallback/pilot path:
- Fast to stand up for on-prem testing. We recommend setting up a pair of HA tunnels into a Transit Gateway (or equivalent) that ACME already uses. This keeps the pilot simple and avoids touching production VPC controls. For production we still prefer PrivateLink for tenant isolation.

Pre-work for workshop (Jordan action items):
- Draft Terraform module snippets for the PrivateLink setup and share by EOD today.
- Provide a short checklist for NetOps (required IAM, S3 bucket policy, endpoint subnets, expected security groups).

We'll review these live on Thursday.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake | Network Architecture Lead
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice Nguyen <alice.nguyen@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Alice — thanks again for taking the time yesterday. As discussed, ACME is preparing to onboard its first private deployment and we want to align on a recommended connectivity pattern that balances latency, operational ownership, and auditability.

I attached a short comparison (redwood-connection-comparison-v2.pdf) but high level the three paths we typically see are: 
- VPC peering (cross-account peering) — low-latency, simple routing but requires both sides to accept peering and route coordination.
- AWS PrivateLink (interface endpoints) — strong isolation and per-service endpoints, easier cross-account access controls, but introduces SNAT/egress considerations depending on your architecture.
- Site-to-site VPN (IPSec) — fast to stand up, supports on-prem and cloud, but higher latency/maintenance and less scalable at high throughput.

Could you confirm any hard constraints from ACME's side (account separation, must-route-through-hub, TLS termination preferences, requirement for flow/audit logs retention)? Once we have that I'll loop in Kimberly and Jordan for the deployment checklist and a pilot plan.

Next step suggested: 60-minute technical workshop this week to pick a primary and fallback path, with roles documented for NetOps and Redwood. Is Thursday 11/19 10:00 PT available?

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
attachments: redwood-connection-comparison-v2.pdf (15 pages), acme-network-requirements-20261116.xlsx (stub)

Ben — thanks for the summary and the docs. Short answers / constraints from NetOps: 
- We cannot accept VPC peering from third-party provider accounts into our production account due to lateral movement policy (we maintain strict account tenancy).
- PrivateLink is acceptable, but we need clarification on who manages the interface endpoints (ACME or Redwood) and how endpoint ENIs are tagged for billing and audit.
- We have an on-prem data center that will need access to inference endpoints for a pilot; we prefer VPN for on-prem -> cloud unless PrivateLink + Transit Gateway is recommended.
- TLS: all traffic must be TLS-terminated at the application layer (no early TLS termination at network edge).
- Logging/retention: flow logs and connection metadata must be retained for 365 days.

Thursday 11/19 10:00 PT works for the workshop. Please include your network architect and an on-call NetOps engineer; I'll invite ours.

title: Execution crosswalk & acceptance checkpoints for MAP-integrated rollout
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@halcyon-analytics.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 09:07:00 -0800
Subject: Execution crosswalk & acceptance checkpoints for MAP-integrated rollout

Rajiv / Elena —

Thanks again for the call earlier. As discussed, attached is our initial execution crosswalk that embeds the post-signature implementation plan directly into the MAP: phased rollout, canary gating, migration windows and the training runway. I built this to align to your internal release cadence and the acceptance metrics your QA team mentioned.

Key asks from our side to keep the timeline tight: 
- Confirm a two-week canary window (we propose 2026-12-01 to 2026-12-14) and whether you can commit a single production tenant for the run.
- Verify acceptance thresholds (see brief below) — we recommend: 99th percentile latency < 600ms, error rate < 0.2%, and zero data-exfil incidents.
- Share a primary contact from your infra/ops for cutover day (timezone PST) and a training roster for two 60-minute sessions the week after canary.

I've included three artifacts: MAP_Execution_Crosswalk_v1.xlsx (detailed milestone matrix), Canary_Exit_Criteria.md (proposed gating checks), and Eval_Promptset_v2.zip (seed prompts for your QA). Please review with your SRE/QA and reply with any critical conflicts.

Next steps I propose: once you confirm canary window and tenant, we’ll schedule a dry-run with our SREs, then a training + runbook handoff 48 hours before the start.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
[jordan.blake@redwood.com]

[Attachment: MAP_Execution_Crosswalk_v1.xlsx (xlsx)]
[Attachment: Canary_Exit_Criteria.md (text)]
[Attachment: Eval_Promptset_v2.zip (zip)]

From: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@halcyon-analytics.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Soto <elena@halcyon-analytics.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 11:22:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Execution crosswalk & acceptance checkpoints for MAP-integrated rollout

Jordan —

Appreciate the quick turnaround and the artifacts. High level we like the structure. A few clarifications from Halcyon ops/QA: 

1) Canary tenant: we can provision a dedicated tenant but only for the first week of your proposed window (2026-12-01 to 2026-12-07). We would require an extension request by Thu 2026-12-03 if we need the second week.

2) Acceptance thresholds: latency and error rate look fine. We will add a functional regression gate: new-response-coherency score (auto-eval against seed prompts) must not drop more than 2.5% vs baseline.

3) Migration windows: our preferred cutover slot is 02:00–04:00 UTC on 2026-12-08 to minimize traffic. Can you confirm Redwood can support rollbacks within that window if the canary fails?

4) Training: we’ll need one live session focused on runbook + incident drills and one recorded walkthrough for developer onboarding. Please include an agenda in the crosswalk doc.

I attached a short QA checklist (we'll push our results back into the map). We also have a legal note: we need audit-level logs for the canary retained for 90 days (is that supported?).

—Rajiv
Head of Platform Engineering | Halcyon Analytics
[Attachment: Halcyon_QA_Checklist_v1.docx (docx)]

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@halcyon-analytics.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 08:34:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Execution crosswalk & acceptance checkpoints for MAP-integrated rollout

Rajiv — thanks, helpful feedback. Quick responses inline: 

1) Canary tenant window: we can start 2026-12-01 as proposed and keep the tenant reserved through 2026-12-14 on Redwood Dedicated with a soft hold — I'll set that as TENTATIVE and we’ll transition to CONFIRMED on sign-off 2026-12-03. If Halcyon needs forced extension after 12/03 we'll honor it per our escalation policy (subject to capacity).

2) Regression gate: adding the coherency delta is fine. I'll surface that as a hard stop in Canary_Exit_Criteria.md and include the automated evaluation script we run against Eval_Promptset_v2.

3) Migration & rollback: yes — rollback within the 02:00–04:00 UTC window is supported. Our standard cutover plan includes an immediate rollback path that takes ~12 minutes to revert to the previous model variant and routing policy; full traffic rehydration back to baseline is expected within 30 minutes. I'll add those SLAs to the crosswalk.

4) Training: proposed agenda (high level) — 60 min live: runbook walkthrough (20m), incident drill & rollback rehearsal (25m), Q&A (15m). Recorded: developer onboarding + integration snippets (30m). We'll add times and a Doodle for availability; suggested live session week of 2026-12-15 PST.

Logs retention: Redwood can retain audit-level canary logs for 90 days. We'll provide an export and secure Drive link; if you need longer retention we can propose an add-on.

Next steps: I will update the crosswalk (tab "Milestones") to include the canary gating as HARD STOP and add the rollback timings and retention notes. Expect a revised workbook by Tue EOD PST. Once Halcyon confirms the tenant window (12/01 start), Vivek and I will schedule the dry-run and the training sessions.

Jordan Blake
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
[jordan.blake@redwood.com]

Thanks again for the demo last week. We're excited to get NorthStar listed in the Redwood Cloud Marketplace. This thread is to capture the technical handoff, operational asks, and timing so both teams are aligned before we open the listing request.

High-level asks from our side (please confirm feasibility):
- Technical: minimal integration for single-sign provisioning + billing attribution tag (see attached MVP spreadsheet). Can Integratech support a lightweight connector (API keys + webhook) within 4–6 weeks?
- Product: we need a clear feature matrix (what's included in the marketplace SKU vs. premium add-ons). Karthik, can you do a short feature mapping?
- Legal/Contract: Redwood requires a Marketplace MSA + standard data processing addendum. If you have counter-redlines, please share at a single consolidated PDF.

I've attached a starter requirements sheet (northstar_mvp_requirements.xlsx) and added our internal Jira link below.

3) Legal exchange of draft MSA redlines by Fri 11/13.

title: Problem: Supplier portal login blocked by cookie banner (SupplyLane)
preview:
From: Emily Cho <emily.cho@supplylane.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: SupplyLane IT <it-support@supplylane.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Unable to complete supplier setup — cookie consent banner blocks login iframe

Hi Grace,

We're trying to finish the vendor setup for the SupplyLane account but hit a blocker on the supplier portal. When we click the 'Accept & Continue' button in the embedded login iframe the cookie consent banner (site-level) appears and overlays the iframe. The iframe's 'Accept' button becomes unresponsive — looks like third-party cookies are blocked by our browser policy.

I attached a screenshot (supplylane-screenshot-iframe.png). We're on Chrome 119.0.6045 on macOS 13 and tried in Safari with the same result. The inflight PO (PO-9921) is pending so this is blocking our procurement team from completing payment setup.

Can Redwood provide a workaround? A one-click top-level activation link would help while you investigate.

Thanks,
Emily Cho
Vendor Ops | SupplyLane, Inc.
attachments: supplylane-screenshot-iframe.png


From: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Emily Cho <emily.cho@supplylane.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 09:38:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unable to complete supplier setup — cookie consent banner blocks login iframe

Hi Emily —

Thanks for the clear repro and the screenshot — sorry for the friction here. I've created an internal ticket (SUP-1228) and looped in Ben (infra) and Jordan (onboard UX).

Quick answers/next steps:
- Ben: can you confirm if the portal iframe is setting any SameSite cookie attributes that would break in an embedded frame? If so, can we issue a short-lived top-level activation URL?
- Emily: as a temporary workaround, if you can open the activation link in a new tab (not the embedded frame) it often bypasses third-party cookie restrictions. I'll send a one-time activation link if we can generate it.

I'll report back within the hour once Ben has a technical read.

Best,
Grace O'Connor
Sr. Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Emily Cho <emily.cho@supplylane.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unable to complete supplier setup — cookie consent banner blocks login iframe

On Thu, Nov 5, 2026 at 9:38 AM Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Emily —
> Thanks for the clear repro and the screenshot — sorry for the friction here. I've created an internal ticket (SUP-1228)...

We've reproduced this in-house. Root cause: the supplier portal uses a top-level consent banner that sets a cookie with SameSite=Lax and additionally relies on a third-party cookie for the embedded auth flow. When the portal is embedded as an iframe (customer sites often do this in procurement UIs) the browser treats the auth cookie as third-party and blocks it; the consent banner is rendered in the parent context and steals focus, making the iframe controls inert.

Temporary remediation we can do immediately:
- Generate a one-time, top-level activation link that opens outside of the iframe and completes the consent flow. This avoids third-party cookie constraints.
- Also sending cookie headers snippet from our reproduction so SupplyLane can share with their IT team; attached as cookie-headers.txt.

Longer-term fixes to propose:
1) Update portal to set SameSite=None; Secure on auth cookies and ensure explicit SameSite=None consent for embedded flows.
2) Provide a small JS postMessage handshake so parent pages can surface the consent banner without stealing focus from the iframe controls.

I'll generate the one-time activation link now and paste it here in a secure HubSpot note (HUB-45321). Grace — want me to issue the link to Emily directly or have you forward?

—Ben
Ben Carter
SRE, Inference Platform
Redwood Inference
attachments: cookie-headers.txt


From: Emily Cho <emily.cho@supplylane.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: SupplyLane IT <it-support@supplylane.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 14:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Unable to complete supplier setup — cookie consent banner blocks login iframe

Hi Grace / Ben —

Thanks — the top-level activation link you provided worked. We opened the link in a new tab and the onboarding finished (PO-9921 now shows as active in our vendor portal). Confirmed with our IT team: they saw the SameSite header in the request and will coordinate to whitelist the Redwood auth cookie or accept the postMessage approach you suggested.

Can you please add SUP-1228 to the roadmap for a permanent fix? We'd like to avoid manual workarounds for future suppliers who embed the portal. HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/45321 (linked above).

Appreciate the quick help.

Best,
Emily Cho
Vendor Ops | SupplyLane, Inc.

title: CloudPO overage guardrails & channel revenue shielding (routing options)
preview:
From: Sam Turner <sam.turner@cloudbuyer.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference>
Date: Sun, 1 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Cloud Marketplace PO — overage guardrails question

Hi Ben,

Quick question as we're finalizing the PO: our procurement team is pushing for explicit overage guardrails on the private offer so we don't get surprise charges during the pilot period. Can you confirm whether Redwood can: 

- Cap overage billing to a percentage of committed spend per month (e.g., +20% cap)
- Emit forecasted usage alerts 48 hours before spikes so our SREs can throttle

Also need clarity on how channel revenue is routed when we buy via the marketplace — will the reseller commission be deducted from the invoice or handled as a separate pass-through?

We're aiming to sign by 11/12 if terms are clear. Attaching our standard PO terms for reference.

Thanks,
Sam Turner
Procurement Manager, CloudBuyer
procurement@brightlane.com

Attachment: BrightLane_PO_Terms.pdf (pdf stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>
To: Sam Turner <sam.turner@cloudbuyer.io>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 2 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cloud Marketplace PO — overage guardrails question

Hi Sam — thanks for the clear ask and timeline. Looping in Irene (ops/legal) for the routing piece; copying Jordan for deal ops. 

Short answers up front:
- Overage cap: we can offer a temporary pilot cap (example: 20% of monthly committed during first 90 days) as a bill-protection clause, subject to renewal terms.
- Forecast alerts: yes — we can push quota/usage webhooks and e-mails 48 hours prior; we will work with your SRE to configure thresholds.

Two channel-routing options we typically propose (pros/cons below):
1) Marketplace pass-through: Redwood invoices BrightLane for list after marketplace takes reseller cut. Pros: cleaner for marketplace accounting. Cons: reseller cut reflected as a reduction on invoice; longer reconciliation.
2) Direct billing + reseller rebate: Redwood invoices BrightLane at net price and issues a separate rebate payment to reseller. Pros: keeps invoice net and predictable; Cons: requires rebate mechanics & partner bookkeeping.

I'm attaching a one-page summary (PO_Term_Summary.xlsx) with the two options and sample numbers. Request: Irene, can you confirm whether legal is comfortable with a short-term 20% cap and which routing approach is preferable from a compliance standpoint?

Next step: if we agree on approach, Jordan can produce a mutual action plan and we can lock dates for the DocuSign.

Best,
Ben Carter
Head of Marketplace Deals — Redwood Inference
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: PO_Term_Summary.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference>
Cc: Sam Turner <sam.turner@cloudbuyer.io>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 08:25:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cloud Marketplace PO — overage guardrails question

Ben / Jordan / Sam — thanks, good framing. A few operational constraints and a recommended safe path: 

Constraints:

Subject: RFP bake-off — credit ask and benchmarking window

Diego,

Thanks again for walking us through Redwood last week. Our procurement team asked me to get crystal-clear on the credit/seat structure for the upcoming RFP bake-off. A few specifics: 

- We need 60 concurrent-worker hours across three weeks (two-week benchmark + one week buffer).
- The bake-off will run side-by-side with two other vendors; procurement expects parity in evaluation resources.
- Can you confirm whether those hours are eligible for promotional top-up credits (separate from standard trial)?

Time is tight — can you send a proposed credit allocation matrix and any ops checklist we should follow to ensure reproducible comparisons?

Appreciate it,
Sarah Liu
Sr. Manager, Vendor Strategy
MedSys Health
[Attachment: MedSys_RFP_Overview.pdf (pdf, 85KB)]

From: Diego Fernandez <diego_fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@medsys.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-04T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: RFP bake-off — credit ask and benchmarking window

Sarah — thanks for the clear ask. Short answer: yes, we can support a conditional top-up to match the bake-off sizing, and we'll include an ops checklist so the tests are apples-to-apples. High-level proposal attached.

What I'm proposing (summary):
- Promotional credit: 300,000 token-equivalent credit + 60 concurrent-worker-hours preloaded to a dedicated sandbox project for three weeks.
- Valid only for the RFP/eval window noted and contingent on a brief mutual action plan and a short SOW (attached).
- We will lock an inference configuration (model version + batch/kv settings) to make the comparison reproducible; ops checklist will list the exact API flags.

Next steps: I need internal signoff on the dollar-equivalent and contractual clause that limits the credit to evaluation use. Looping in internal ops & procurement for approvals. If that looks good, I'll send a DocuSign for the SOW and provision the sandbox.

Best,
Diego Fernandez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: credit-allocation-matrix.xlsx (xls, 24KB)]

Date: 2026-11-04T13:22:00-07:00
Subject: INTERNAL: MedSys RFP — request for approval (credits + ops)

3) Commercial impact: approximate list-price equivalent ~$9k; recommend approval under promotional pool with standard refund clawback if violated.

Customer expectation: parity with competitors — if we delay provisioning or require onerous data-sharing, MedSys may favor the competitor. This is strategic win-opportunity (healthcare prospect).

Pls reply with one-line approval or edits so I can confirm with Sarah this afternoon.

— Diego

From: Susan Lee <susan@blueridge.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: accounts-payable@blueridge.com
Date: Sun, 1 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Invoice intake question for Redwood

Hi Dylan,

Quick question as we're getting our vendor account set up with Redwood: what's your preferred route for invoice submission — a vendor portal or direct email to AP? Our billing team usually sends a PDF invoice to accounts-payable@redwood.ai, but we can adapt if the portal is required.

I'm attaching a sample invoice so you can confirm whether fields look OK for your system. Also: do you need PO stamping on the invoice, or is a PO number in the body enough?

Thanks —
Susan Lee
Accounts Payable Manager
Blue Ridge Supply Co.
[Attachment: BR_Sample_Invoice.pdf (PDF, 1 page)]

From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Susan Lee <susan@blueridge.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 1 Nov 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Invoice intake question for Redwood

Hi Susan — thanks for the sample. Short answer: we prefer vendor portal onboarding where possible (better ACKs, automated PO match), but we support email fallback for legacy suppliers. A couple of specifics below so you can pick the cleanest path: 

- Portal: Preferred. It gives an AP acknowledgment (invoice ID) and prevents duplicate submissions. If you can register, use the portal link (I sent the onboarding invite link in a separate email).
- Email fallback: acceptable for <100 invoices/mo or exceptional cases. If you use email, please put the PO number in the subject line using this convention: PO-<number>_BR-<vendorid> (example: PO-17392_BR-1972). Body should include invoice total and currency on first 3 lines.
- PO stamping: Not mandatory if the PO number is clearly present; stamping helps automated OCR but is not required.

Grace / Jordan — can you confirm the required minimum fields and whether we need vendor-side line-item CSV in addition to PDF for faster ingestion?

Best,
Dylan
Manager, Vendor Onboarding — Redwood Inference

Dylan / Jordan — looping in procurement rules. For Blue Ridge specifically, procurement's minimum intake schema is below. If vendor can supply a small line-item CSV alongside the PDF that will eliminate most manual work. Otherwise PDF-only is acceptable but expect a slower ACK and potential manual mapping.

Minimum required fields (must be machine-parseable):
- Redwood PO number (format: PO-######)
- Invoice number (unique per vendor)
- Invoice date (ISO: YYYY-MM-DD)
- Invoice total and currency (e.g., 2,345.00 USD)
- Bill-to Redwood legal entity (we'll provide exact wording in portal invite)

Preferred (speeds processing):
- Line-item rows as CSV with: description, quantity, unit_price, line_total, tax_amount, account_code
- Attachment: single PDF invoice named using: BR-INV-<vendorid>-<invoice#>.pdf

I've attached the vendor onboarding form (v2) — please complete Section B (banking and remit info) and Section D (billing contact + sample invoice examples). If Blue Ridge registers in the portal we will auto-create the vendor record and send a confirmation.

If email fallback is used, please:
- Send to invoices@redwood.ai (not accounts-payable@)
- Put PO in subject as Dylan suggested
- Include invoice as PDF attachment (no zipped batches)

Attachments: vendor_onboarding_form_v2.docx

Head of Procurement — Redwood Inference

Hi Susan — thanks for the sample. Short answer: we prefer vendor portal onboarding where possible (better ACKs, automated PO match), but we support email fallback for legacy suppliers. A couple of specifics below so you can pick the cleanest path: 

- Portal: Preferred. It gives an AP acknowledgment (invoice ID) and prevents duplicate submissions. If you can register, use the portal link (I sent the onboarding invite link in a separate email).
- Email fallback: acceptable for <100 invoices/mo or exceptional cases. If you use email, please put the PO number in the subject line using this convention: PO-<number>_BR-<vendorid> (example: PO-17392_BR-1972). Body should include invoice total and currency on first 3 lines.
- PO stamping: Not mandatory if the PO number is clearly present; stamping helps automated OCR but is not required.

Grace / Jordan — can you confirm the required minimum fields and whether we need vendor-side line-item CSV in addition to PDF for faster ingestion?

Best,
Dylan
Manager, Vendor Onboarding — Redwood Inference

Dylan / Jordan — looping in procurement rules. For Blue Ridge specifically, procurement's minimum intake schema is below. If vendor can supply a small line-item CSV alongside the PDF that will eliminate most manual work. Otherwise PDF-only is acceptable but expect a slower ACK and potential manual mapping.

Minimum required fields (must be machine-parseable):
- Redwood PO number (format: PO-######)
- Invoice number (unique per vendor)
- Invoice date (ISO: YYYY-MM-DD)
- Invoice total and currency (e.g., 2,345.00 USD)
- Bill-to Redwood legal entity (we'll provide exact wording in portal invite)

Preferred (speeds processing):
- Line-item rows as CSV with: description, quantity, unit_price, line_total, tax_amount, account_code
- Attachment: single PDF invoice named using: BR-INV-<vendorid>-<invoice#>.pdf

I've attached the vendor onboarding form (v2) — please complete Section B (banking and remit info) and Section D (billing contact + sample invoice examples). If Blue Ridge registers in the portal we will auto-create the vendor record and send a confirmation.

If email fallback is used, please:
- Send to invoices@redwood.ai (not accounts-payable@)
- Put PO in subject as Dylan suggested
- Include invoice as PDF attachment (no zipped batches)

Attachments: vendor_onboarding_form_v2.docx

Grace O'Connor
Head of Procurement — Redwood Inference
